docs/bead-backends.md:3:This document describes the bd/DoltDB fallback path for bead storage if the axon rollout stalls or is deferred. It is the practical wiring guide for preserving the `Backend` contract introduced in `ddx-bbdd7564` while contrasting that path with the Axon backend design in [TD-030](helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md).
docs/bead-backends.md:10:- It includes CRUD, claim, list/ready/blocked, dependency operations, event append, archive split, and JSONL import/export.
docs/bead-backends.md:19:That separation is what lets DDx keep JSONL as a safe default while swapping in bd, br, or axon behavior without changing call sites.
docs/bead-backends.md:34:The axon path is defined in `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:15-105` and selected in `cli/internal/bead/store.go:127-137`.
docs/bead-backends.md:38:- The current implementation is an in-process emulation that persists two JSONL collections under `.ddx/axon/`.
docs/bead-backends.md:40:That makes axon a feature-flagged, repository-local implementation path. The bd fallback is different:
docs/bead-backends.md:44:- bd uses the same JSONL interchange contract for import/export.
docs/bead-backends.md:49:If axon adoption stalls and bd becomes the preferred backend, a future implementer should follow these steps:
docs/bead-backends.md:65:- import/export remains a shell-out boundary
docs/bead-backends.md:68:That is the main difference from axon in this repository:
docs/bead-backends.md:70:- axon is self-contained inside DDx but is still experimental
docs/bead-backends.md:75:If axon remains unstable, prefer this ordering:
docs/bead-backends.md:79:3. Keep axon behind `DDX_AXON_EXPERIMENTAL` until its conformance and rollout are proven.

docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:116:- **Default path:** `.ddx/beads.jsonl` (one JSON object per line, sorted by id)
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:119:- **Write algorithm:** Mutating operations take the lock, build a complete bead snapshot, write to `beads.jsonl.tmp`, and atomically rename the temp file into place. This avoids partially written tracker state.
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:120:- **Read algorithm:** Queue commands scan `beads.jsonl` line-by-line. Valid JSON objects are loaded into a snapshot; malformed lines are skipped with line-numbered warnings so one bad record does not take down the entire queue.
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:121:- **Repair algorithm:** If a read finds malformed lines but at least one valid bead, DDx takes the store lock, copies the current file to `.ddx/beads.jsonl.bak`, and rewrites the repaired snapshot atomically.
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:123:- **Git integration:** `beads.jsonl` is project state and must be committed to git. Auto-commit behavior after bead mutations is defined in FEAT-012 (Git Awareness), requirements S7 and US-124. `ddx init` generates agent guidance that includes `beads.jsonl` as a committable file (FEAT-012 S8, US-125).

.ddx/attachments/ddx-fdd3ea36/events.jsonl:3:{"actor":"ddx","body":"no_changes\nrationale: # No-changes rationale — ddx-fdd3ea36 (routing point release adoption)\n\n## Decision\n\nStopping without a commit. This bead's acceptance criteria describe a\nmulti-PR release-adoption epic, not a single bounded change. Attempting it\nin one execute-bead pass would produce either (a) a partial commit that\nfails most AC items, or (b) a sprawling change touching dispatch, config\nschema, CLI surface, frontend Playwright fixtures, and CHANGELOG/docs\nwithout the up-front design alignment the bead itself calls for.\n\n## What is already done (verified in this worktree)\n\n1. **Upstream routing-plan beads are all closed.** All six beads under\n   label `routing-plan` in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl` are in\n   `closed` status:\n     - agent-0dafc7f0 (cost-aware routing)\n     - agent-191a74f9 (default + local + standard profiles)\n     - agent-1f46cf22 (routing docs)\n     - agent-53f38d95 (public decision trace)\n     - agent-bab52778 (supported-models allow-list)\n     - agent-dfabb10b (typed errors)\n\n2. **Their closing commits are in DDx's currently-pinned upstream tag.**\n   `cli/go.mod` references `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.9.23`.\n   `git tag --contains` for each routing-plan closing commit returns\n   v0.9.10 as the earliest containing tag. v0.9.23 (and v0.9.24 latest)\n   transitively contain all of them. So the *binary surface* AC #1\n   wants is already accessible via the pinned go.mod.\n\n## What is NOT done — and why this can't be a one-shot pass\n\n### AC #1 — CHANGELOG-as-released-narrative\n\nAC #1 asks for \"a ddx-agent version whose CHANGELOG documents: first-class\ndefault and local profiles; supported-models allow-list in routing inputs +\ngating; ErrHarnessModelIncompatible; public decision-trace …; cost\ndimension on ProviderEntry with unknown-cost fallback.\"\n\nReading `~/Projects/agent/CHANGELOG.md`:\n  - v0.9.10 documents prompt-caching work, not the routing-plan work.\n  - v0.9.11 documents ADR-006 override-as-failure-signal.\n  - v0.9.12-v0.9.24 cover lucebox/vllm, conformance, corpus, terminal-bench,\n    pi-runner panic. None of these releases bundles a coherent\n    \"routing point release\" narrative.\n\nThe closing commits for the six beads landed scattered between tags as\nside-quest work; none of those tags' release notes call out the\ncombined contract DDx now wants to consume. AC #1 effectively requires\neither (a) an upstream re-release that publishes a \"v0.9.25 — routing\npoint release\" entry covering exactly those items, OR (b) DDx's bead\ndescription must be relaxed to accept \"the code is in v0.9.23, even if\nno single CHANGELOG block summarizes it.\" Either resolution is a\ngovernance decision, not a coding task this worktree can execute.\n\n### AC #2-7 — DDx-side surface (D1-D5)\n\nThese criteria collectively require:\n  - rewriting the worker dispatch path so the default issues exactly one\n    `ResolveRoute(Profile: \"default\")` call with no Model / no Harness /\n    no Provider, removing all DDx-side ladder synthesis;\n  - inserting a typed-incompatibility preflight gate before bead claim\n    (`execute_bead_loop.go` ~line 341);\n  - making `agent.routing.default_harness` a hard error at config load,\n    `profile_ladders` and `model_overrides` opt-in via `--escalate`/`--override-model`,\n    with one-time process warnings;\n  - guaranteeing override flag combinations route through unchanged to\n    upstream and surface upstream typed errors;\n  - removing `ResolveProfileLadder` / `ResolveTierModelRef` /\n    `workersByHarness` escalation helpers from the default path with\n    dead-code detection in CI;\n  - adding an integration test that reproduces the historical 19-burn\n    drain-queue bug AND new e2e tests covering each override\n    combination (claude+opus, model-only, harness-only, profile+harness\n    conflict);\n  - adding Playwright drain-queue golden fixtures matching the user's\n    real config;\n  - updating CHANGELOG and docs.\n\nThis is on the order of 1-3k LOC delta across:\n  - `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`\n  - `cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go`\n  - `cli/internal/agent/dispatch.go`\n  - `cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go`\n  - `cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go` (workerDispatchAdapter)\n  - `cli/internal/config/*` (deprecated field schema)\n  - `cli/cmd/agent_*.go` (override flag plumbing)\n  - new tests (unit, integration, regression, Playwright)\n\nThat work spans D1-D5 in the bead's own sequencing list, which the bead\nexplicitly bundles into \"one DDx PR\" — but a PR of that size needs\narchitectural alignment with the user (especially around the breaking\nconfig change in D4 and the dead-code detector in AC #7) before the\nfirst line is written. execute-bead's contract is to satisfy the AC\ndeterministically, not to make architecture decisions.\n\n### AC #8 — D6 / D7 follow-up beads\n\nThe bead requires that follow-up beads be filed:\n  - one for the route-status / providers-UI decision-trace consumer,\n  - acknowledgement that `ddx-05b4cc9d` (workersByProject filter)\n    stays independent.\n\nFiling beads requires `ddx bead create` invocations against the\nproject's tracker and a commit that mutates `.ddx/beads.jsonl`. This is\nmechanical but should ride along with the substantive PR, not a no-op.\n\n### AC #10 — drain-queue regression golden\n\nA net-new integration test that reproduces the user's historical\n19-burn failure with their real config. Designing this test\n(particularly the gemini-binary-installed assertion, since CI may not\nhave gemini available) is itself a sub-bead.\n\n## What a follow-up attempt needs\n\nTo make this bead landable in a single execute-bead pass, the bead\nmust be split into smaller beads, each of which is a well-defined PR:\n\n1. **routing-bump** — bump `cli/go.mod` from v0.9.23 to whatever\n   tag (v0.9.25?) the upstream cuts as the routing point release; add\n   DDx CHANGELOG entry citing upstream release notes. Trivially\n   satisfies AC #1 once upstream cuts the tag.\n\n2. **routing-default-path** — D1 + D2 only. Worker dispatch + default\n   `ddx agent run` and `ddx work` issue `ResolveRoute(Profile:\n   \"default\")`. New unit tests. Does not touch config schema yet.\n\n3. **routing-preflight-gate** — D3 only. Insert preflight before\n   `Claim` at `execute_bead_loop.go:~341`. Test with seeded\n   incompatibility.\n\n4. **routing-config-deprecation** — D4 only. Hard error on\n   `default_harness`, opt-in warnings on others. Migration test +\n   CHANGELOG breaking-change note.\n\n5. **routing-overrides-e2e** — D5 + AC #5 e2e tests for each override\n   combination.\n\n6. **routing-drain-regression** — AC #10. Standalone regression test\n   that reproduces the historical 19-burn bug.\n\n7. **routing-cleanup** — AC #7 dead-code detector + removal of\n   compensating logic.\n\n8. **routing-frontend-goldens** — AC #6 Playwright fixtures.\n\n9. **routing-followups** — AC #8 file the route-status / providers-UI\n   bead.\n\nEach of those is a clean execute-bead pass with provable AC. The\ncurrent bead's AC list is the union of all nine — too broad for one\nattempt.\n\n## Recommendation to the orchestrator\n\nReopen this bead with `helix-evolve` (or equivalent) to split it into\nthe nine sub-beads above, then queue them in order. The first three\n(bump + default-path + preflight-gate) unblock the user's stated\ngoals (G1/G2/G3) in the smallest possible PR.\n\nThe reduced-scope language in the bead's own description (\"DDx-side\ntracker only\", \"blocks on a single upstream point release\") suggests\nthis bead was meant as the EPIC, not the deliverable. Treating it as\nthe deliverable in execute-bead is what produced this no-op outcome.\nresult_rev=891112dee30444c8925bbc101abd3b1da50a3441\nbase_rev=891112dee30444c8925bbc101abd3b1da50a3441\nretry_after=2026-04-29T04:30:22Z","created_at":"2026-04-28T22:30:22.575970819Z","kind":"execute-bead","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"no_changes"}

The changelog\n    explicitly notes that upstream shipped routing capabilities incrementally\n    across v0.9.10..v0.9.24 rather than as a single tag.\n  * All six upstream beads labeled `routing-plan` in\n    ~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl are CLOSED:\n      agent-0dafc7f0 (cost-aware tiebreak)\n      agent-191a74f9 (first-class default + local + standard profiles)\n      agent-1f46cf22 (routing reference docs)\n      agent-53f38d95 (public RouteDecision.Candidates trace)\n      agent-bab52778 (supported-models allow-list)\n      agent-dfabb10b (typed ErrHarnessModelIncompatible / ErrProfilePinConflict)\n  * The needed public types are present in the pinned module at\n    /home/erik/go/pkg/mod/github.com/!document!driven!d!x/agent@v0.9.23/routing_errors.go:\n      ErrHarnessModelIncompatible, ErrProfilePinConflict, ErrNoProfileCandidate,\n      ErrUnknownProfile, ErrNoLiveProvider, DecisionWithCandidates interface\n      with RouteCandidates() []RouteCandidate.\n\n(b) WHAT IS BLOCKING THIS ATTEMPT\n=================================\nThe bead is an epic-tracker, not a single deliverable. AC #2 through #10\ncollectively require — in one PR with no compensating paths retained — all of:\n\n  D1. workerDispatchAdapter.DispatchWorker rewrite + Drain-button parity.\n  D2. Removal of default-path tier-ladder synthesis from\n      ddx agent run / ddx agent execute-bead / ddx work; ResolveProfileLadder\n      and ResolveTierModelRef gated behind --escalate only.\n  D3. Preflight gate inserted before beadStore.Claim in execute_bead_loop.go\n      (the bead points at line 352, which has already drifted — the actual\n      Claim site is now line 341; the loop is 1052 lines and has substantial\n      surrounding dispatch state to thread through).\n  D4. CLI surface parity for ddx agent run (no flags / --profile / --harness /\n      --model / combos), ddx work, ddx agent execute-bead.\n\n  Plus tests demanded by the AC:\n  - AC #2 integration test reproducing the original 19-burn drain failure\n    against the user's real config shape.\n  - AC #3 worker-level failure record assertion with seeded pathological route\n    via an upstream test seam that does not currently exist on the DDx side.\n  - AC #5 four end-to-end override-behavior tests (incl. cost-tiebreak\n    seeded-providers test).\n  - AC #6 Playwright golden for the drain-queue UI path\n    (cli/internal/server/frontend); fixture must mirror user's real\n    endpoint config.\n  - AC #7 dead-code detector wired into CI catching orphaned ladder helpers.\n  - AC #10 regression-guard integration test that fails before this work and\n    succeeds after.\n\n  Plus the directive in AC #7 — \"Search DDx for code that re-implements any\n  of: allow-list checks, exact-pin filtering, profile-to-preference mapping,\n  provider cost scoring. Must be absent. If found, PR fails review.\" —\n  requires a deliberate audit + removal pass spanning at least:\n      cli/internal/agent/harness_config.go\n      cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go\n      cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder_test.go\n      cli/internal/config/config.go\n      cli/internal/config/types.go\n      cli/internal/config/routing_ladder_test.go\n      cli/internal/config/schema/config.schema.json\n      cli/internal/server/workers.go\n      cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go\n      cli/cmd/agent_run_profile_test.go\n      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (1052 lines)\n      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go\n      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_runwithconfig_test.go\n      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_resolvers_e2e_test.go\n      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_pin_propagation_test.go\n\nThis is a multi-thousand-line, multi-subsystem refactor with a hard\nbreaking-change config migration and a new Playwright golden. It cannot be\nlanded responsibly in a single execute-bead pass without a much narrower\nsub-bead breakdown. Attempting it as one commit risks: half-finished tier-\nladder removal that leaves the default path in an undefined state; a config\nschema break that hard-errors existing user configs without the migration\nwarnings actually firing; or merging a Playwright golden whose fixture\nsilently passes because the underlying dispatch path was not fully rewired.\n\nThe previous attempt already correctly scoped this bead to AC #1 only and\ndocumented the upstream prerequisite. Unit test +\n                dashboard wiring. Closes AC #2 partially.\n\n  ddx-<new>-D2a: Default path stops calling ResolveTierModelRef /\n                 ResolveProfileLadder. Closes AC #3.\n\n  ddx-<new>-D4: Config breaking-change migration. Hard-error on\n                default_harness; warn-and-ignore on profile_ladders/\n                model_overrides; schema update; migration fixture tests;\n                CHANGELOG migration guide. Closes AC #4.\n\n  ddx-<new>-D5: CLI flag-surface parity tests for ddx agent run / work /\n                execute-bead with the four override permutations from AC #5.\n                Closes AC #5.\n\n  ddx-<new>-Audit: Audit pass for AC #7 (compensating logic removal +\n                   dead-code orphan detector in CI). Closes AC #6.\n\n  ddx-<new>-Docs: Cross-link DDx CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md to upstream profile\n                  catalog + override precedence + best-provider contract\n                  docs (AC #9 second half — CHANGELOG half is already in\n                  CHANGELOG.md from the prior commit).\n\n  ddx-<new>-D6-followup: Decision-trace consumers in route-status /\n                         providers UI — explicitly out of scope per the\n                         bead description but should be filed now so the\n                         tracking exists (bead description's own AC #8\n                         requires this).\n\nddx-fdd3ea36 should remain open as the umbrella tracker until all\nsub-beads close, at which point AC #2..#10 can be re-verified end-to-end\nin one final review pass.\n\nNO CODE CHANGES IN THIS ATTEMPT.\nresult_rev=3dc94e0a7f4cf67a86bd4583757f7263aa957568\nbase_rev=3dc94e0a7f4cf67a86bd4583757f7263aa957568\nretry_after=2026-04-29T06:33:06Z","created_at":"2026-04-29T00:33:06.128992877Z","kind":"execute-bead","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"no_changes"}

Active-collection guard at line 168\n    prevents recursion when the caller is already on the archive collection.\n  - Test: TestArchiveGetWithArchiveFallsBack\n    (cli/internal/bead/archive_test.go:150-183) seeds an active and an\n    archived bead, runs Archive(), and asserts GetWithArchive resolves both\n    IDs and errors on a missing one. PASS.\n\nAC3. ddx bead list has a --include-archive flag (or equivalent) and the\ndefault behavior is documented.\n\n  - cli/cmd/bead.go:846 declares the equivalent flag:\n    cmd.Flags().Bool(\"all\", false, \"Include archived beads\n    (.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl)\").\n  - Default behavior is to consult the archive partner via ListWithArchive\n    (bead.go:808-812). The flag is a no-op kept for compatibility, with the\n    behavior documented in the inline comment at bead.go:808-810.\n  - Implementation: archive.go:182-209 — union with active-wins precedence,\n    archive-missing tolerated as empty.\n  - Test: TestArchiveListWithArchiveIncludesBoth (archive_test.go:216-245)\n    exercises both the active-only List() and the merged ListWithArchive().\n\nAC4. ddx bead ready / blocked still operate only on the active collection.\n\n  - cli/internal/bead/store.go:1224-1344 — Ready() / ReadyExecution() route\n    through readyFiltered() which calls s.ReadAll(), the active-collection\n    read. The archive partner is never consulted.\n  - cli/internal/bead/store.go:1347-1469 — Blocked() / BlockedAll() also\n    call s.ReadAll(); no archive read-through.\n  - Test: TestArchiveReadyAndBlockedQueryActiveOnly\n    (archive_test.go:185-214) seeds a closed-and-archived bead alongside an\n    open active bead, runs Archive() so the closed entry truly leaves the\n    active file, then asserts Ready() returns only the open bead and\n    Blocked() is empty. PASS.\n\nAC5. ddx work / agent execute-loop ignores archive when picking next work.\n\n  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:199 declares the worker's Store\n    interface as ReadyExecution()-only.\n  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1088 — w.Store.ReadyExecution()\n    is the queue source, which (per AC4) runs against the active collection\n    only.\n  - The same readyFiltered active-only ReadAll covers this path; no\n    additional plumbing reaches into the archive partner from the loop.\n\nAC6. New tests cover read-through for show, and active-only behavior for\nready/blocked.\n\n  - Read-through for show: TestArchiveGetWithArchiveFallsBack\n    (archive_test.go:150).\n  - Active-only behavior for ready/blocked:\n    TestArchiveReadyAndBlockedQueryActiveOnly (archive_test.go:185).\n  - Supporting coverage: TestArchiveMovesEligibleClosedBeads (line 36),\n    TestArchivePreservesReferencedClosedBeads (line 119),\n    TestArchiveListWithArchiveIncludesBoth (line 216),\n    TestArchiveOpportunisticTriggerOnClose (line 247).\n\nVerification\n------------\n\n  cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/...   → ok (4.933s)\n  cd cli && go test ./cmd/ -run TestBead  → ok (1.617s)\n\nWhy nothing was committed\n-------------------------\n\nThe bead description treats archive read-through as net-new work building on\nddx-2f453147. The earlier slice in this ADR-004 decomposition already shipped\nthe registry, the archive partner store, GetWithArchive / ListWithArchive,\nthe `bead show` and `bead list` wiring in cli/cmd/bead.go, the active-only\nbehavior of Ready/Blocked/ReadyExecution (which falls out of using ReadAll\non the active store), and the matching tests in archive_test.go. There is\nno remaining gap to fill for any of the six AC items, so committing\nadditional code or tests would be churn rather than progress.\n\nIf a future review disagrees and identifies a specific AC item that is in\nfact unmet, the natural next slice would be to add the missing piece (e.g.\na fully-isolated `--include-archive` flag distinct from the legacy `--all`\nno-op, or a dedicated cmd-level integration test for `bead show` against an\narchived ID). ParseReviewVerdict accepts: raw JSON; ```json …``` fenced JSON; mixed prose+JSON where the JSON is the last fenced block. The reviewer prompt template (in execute_bead_review.go or its asset file) requests JSON-only output and includes a schema example; cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test -run TestReviewPromptRequestsJSON ./internal/agent/... passes by string-matching the template.\n7. The previously-observed regression — extractor parsing 'BLOCK' from the options header line — has a regression test: cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test -run TestReviewerNoOptionsHeaderRegression ./internal/agent/... feeds a transcript that includes only the prompt template's options header echoed back and asserts the parser rejects it (unparseable), not parses it as BLOCK.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-25T09:24:10Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"196235","closing_commit_sha":"ed2219bf951ce9b7770ed684fb6c789a51b3344d","created_at":"2026-04-25T08:00:51.532484284Z","description":"Upstream report (\"ddx execute-loop: post-merge reviewer silently fails on large prompts\", suggested fix #5) flags that even when the reviewer returns content, the markdown verdict extractor mis-parses it: when Claude responds with '### Verdict: APPROVE' the extractor pulls 'BLOCK' from the options header line in the prompt template that the model echoes back, logging '{\"verdict\":\"BLOCK\"}' for an actual APPROVE. This silently lets regressions land.\n\nFEAT-022 (parent ddx-0c35470e) is scoped to prompt assembly and size invariants; response parsing is out of scope there. File this as a sibling.\n\nSwitch the reviewer contract to a machine-readable JSON object emitted as the model's final response:\n\n  {\n    \"verdict\": \"APPROVE\" | \"REQUEST_CHANGES\" | \"BLOCK\",\n    \"findings\": [\n      { \"severity\": \"info\"|\"warn\"|\"block\", \"summary\": \"…\", \"location\": \"path:line?\" }\n    ],\n    \"summary\": \"≤300 char human-readable verdict justification\"\n  }\n\nReplace the existing markdown extractor with a strict JSON parser:\n1. Update the reviewer prompt template (post-collapse: agent.BuildReviewPrompt) to instruct the model to emit ONLY a JSON object matching the schema, fenced as ```json … ``` for robustness against models that won't emit raw JSON.\n2. Add internal/agent/review_verdict.go with a typed ReviewVerdict struct and a ParseReviewVerdict([]byte) (ReviewVerdict, error) that strips an optional ```json fence and decodes; reject on unknown verdict values.\n3. Replace the regex-based extractor call site in execute_bead_review.go with ParseReviewVerdict; on parse error, emit the existing 'unparseable' review-error class (introduced by ddx-70c1d2e2) — do NOT close the bead.\n4. Schema-version the contract: include schema_version: 1 in the prompt; the parser tolerates unknown fields but rejects unknown verdict values.\n\nIn-scope files:\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go (prompt template, extractor call site)\n- cli/internal/agent/review_verdict.go (new)\n- cli/internal/agent/review_verdict_test.go (new)\n- prompt template asset under cli/internal/agent/assets/ if separated\n\nOut of scope:\n- Bounded retry / error-class taxonomy (ddx-70c1d2e2 owns this; this bead emits 'unparseable' into that taxonomy)\n- Prompt size invariants (FEAT-022)\n- Grading verdicts (separate bead if needed; this is review-only)\n\nCross-reference: This bead's `unparseable` emission feeds into ddx-70c1d2e2's bounded-retry logic. ddx-70c1d2e2 should be reviewed once both land to confirm the unparseable retry budget is sane (the upstream report observed real APPROVE verdicts being lost, so unparseable should retry, not terminal-escalate immediately).","events_attachment":"ddx-021bd69b/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-25T09:39:11.049944919Z","id":"ddx-021bd69b","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","kind:implementation","area:agent","area:review"],"owner":"ddx","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-b2bae304","status":"closed","title":"review: machine-readable JSON verdict contract + replace markdown extractor","updated_at":"2026-04-25T09:40:01.996055932Z"}

Linked from skills/ddx/SKILL.md intent-router.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-17T22:05:31.000159751Z","description":"Create skills/ddx/reference/beads.md — the domain reference file a harness reads when the user asks to create, plan, or update beads.\n\nContent required:\n- **Inline context, not links.** Every bead description must be readable cold by an automated execute-bead worker that has no chat history and no open tabs.\n- **Explicit in-scope file list.** Repo-relative paths. Bad: 'the auth module'. Good: 'cli/internal/auth/pipeline.go and cli/internal/auth/pipeline_test.go'.\n- **Explicit do-NOT-touch scope negation** (non-goals). Prevents scope creep during agent execution.\n- **Favor many small beads.** Rule of thumb: if a bead would touch more than ~3 files or more than one subsystem, decompose into child beads under an epic.\n- **Acceptance is a command that passes, not a sentence.** 'X works' is not AC. 'cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/foo/... passes and returns 0' is AC.\n- **Implementations include tests.** The acceptance criteria must reference a test command.\n- **Rollback/cleanup expectations** for partial changes — what 'leave it unclaimed' means in practice.\n- **spec-id linkage** when a governing artifact exists (FEAT-*, SD-*, TD-*, ADR-*); omit when it doesn't.\n- **CLI reference** at the end: ddx bead create/update/close/list/show/ready/blocked/dep/import/export/evidence.\n\nSource material: existing /skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md (already contains much of this — especially the top-level \"Execution-Ready\" section added in an earlier refinement). Strip HELIX-specific rules: remove the mandatory 'helix' label and the phase:frame|design|test|build|deploy|iterate|review enumeration. Keep generic guidance ('label beads with area, kind, and any workflow tags your project uses').\n\nIn-scope files:\n- skills/ddx/reference/beads.md (new)\n\nOut-of-scope:\n- skills/ddx/SKILL.md (separate bead ddx-bd852e86)\n- skills/ddx/reference/{work,review,agents,status}.md (separate beads)","id":"ddx-0827da45","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:implementation","area:skills"],"parent":"ddx-f586b4fd","priority":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-011","status":"closed","title":"Create skills/ddx/reference/beads.md — execution-ready bead authoring","updated_at":"2026-04-17T22:23:03.141018211Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:61:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer reviewing how different harness/provider/model combinations are performing, I expect the Efficacy page to load quickly on a workspace with tens of thousands of sessions, and I expect the rows to reflect every agent invocation — not just those tied to closed beads.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Source change.** `EfficacyRows` resolver reads from the sharded sessions index (`.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl`, per `ddx-2ceb02fa`). Bead-event read paths (`efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot`'s event loop, `attemptsFromEvidence`, `efficacyMemo`) are removed in the same PR — no dead code retained.\n\n2. **Schema parity.** Row shape and field names stay as they are today (rowKey, harness, provider, model, attempts, successes, successRate, medianInputTokens, medianOutputTokens, medianDurationMs, medianCostUsd, warning). UI does not need to change. Optional new filters (`since`, `until`, `projectId`) are added as nullable args with sensible defaults.\n\n3. **Fixture + baseline recorded.** Fixture seeds ≥10,000 session index rows across 2+ monthly shards with ≥10 distinct `(harness, provider, model)` groups. User feedback: efficacy should really be \"an aggregation of sessions grouped by provider / harness / model\" — which is not what the current implementation does.\n\n## Ground truth (current implementation)\n\n`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:676-833` —\n\n- `EfficacyRows` builds its rollup from **bead evidence events** (`kind:routing` and `kind:cost` on every closed bead), not from sessions.\n- Computation path: `store.ReadAll()` → for every closed bead, read its event file → filter for routing/cost → per-(harness, provider, model) aggregate.\n- There is a cache (`efficacyMemo`), keyed by a fingerprint that itself walks every event on every closed bead to compute event count + max timestamp. So cache validation costs almost as much as cache rebuild.\n- Cost shape: `O(closed_beads × events_per_bead)` on every request (cache miss) and even on cache hit the fingerprint walk happens.\n\n## Why this is wrong (per user feedback)\n\n- Efficacy is a sessions-view concept: \"how is harness/provider/model X doing?\" Sessions are the natural row. Bead events are a lossy, indirect proxy — they only exist for *closed* beads, they duplicate session state, and they force coupling between the efficacy feature and bead lifecycle.\n- The bead-event path misses: open beads with in-flight sessions, sessions that didn't close a bead, benchmark/quorum sessions, non-execute-bead invocations (e.g., `ddx agent run`).\n- The sessions feed is already being reworked into a sharded pointer index (`ddx-2ceb02fa`). Efficacy should read from that index — single source of truth.\n\n## Proposed direction\n\n### Part 1 — Reshape the data source\n\n- Rebuild `EfficacyRows` as an aggregation over `sessions.index` (the sharded index from `ddx-2ceb02fa`), not over bead events.\n- Grouping key: `(harness, provider, model)` — optionally filterable by project, bead, label, time range.\n- Metrics per row: attempts (count), successes (count + rate), median input/output tokens, median duration, median cost. Measures steady-state behavior as the index grows.\n- Baseline captured in the bead notes before any optimization.\n\n### Part 3 — Perf targets (first pass, in-place)\n\n- Default Efficacy view (all-time) on a 10k-session, 2-shard fixture: p95 ≤ 150ms in-process / ≤ 400ms HTTP.\n- Date-filtered view (last 30 days, 1 shard): p95 ≤ 60ms in-process / ≤ 200ms HTTP.\n- If in-place optimization (scope, pushdown, memoize-per-shard, streaming aggregation) **cannot hit these numbers** on the 50k stretch fixture, **do not silently change substrate** — raise the decision with the user. .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:66:{"archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"7d18cb4a228d9c3d6a8d3f381e34801b2cd7da2c","created_at":"2026-04-15T01:14:36.326164034Z","description":"'ddx agent execute-loop' help says 'execute-loop scans the current project's execution-ready bead queue.' When submitting to a multi-project server, there is no way to say 'run this loop against project X'. The server picks whichever project is the current server context, ignoring the caller's CWD.\n\n## Observed\n\nFrom CWD /Users/erik/Projects/axon:\n\n  $ ddx agent execute-loop --once --harness claude --model claude-sonnet-4-6 --json\n  {\n    \"project_root\": \"/Users/erik/Projects/ddx\",  # WRONG\n    \"state\": \"running\",\n    \"worker_id\": \"worker-20260415T011331-d0a4\"\n  }\n\nI wanted the worker to pick an axon bead. The server ran the worker against the ddx repo instead. There is no --project flag, no env var, nothing in the CLI to redirect the server's project scope.\n\nCurrently the only way to target a specific project with execute-loop is --local mode (which spawns a subprocess using the caller's CWD as the project root). This defeats the purpose of having a server in a multi-project deployment.\n\n## Scope\n\n- --project \u003cpath\u003e or --project \u003cname\u003e flag on execute-loop, execute-bead, and 'ddx agent run' that tells the server which project_root to use\n- Env var fallback: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT or similar\n- Default to CWD if unset and no flag\n- Server refuses ambiguous/unmatched project names with a clean error\n- Integration test: from CWD A, submit a loop targeting project B; assert worker runs in B's project_root\n\n## Why this matters for the user's workflow\n\nWithout this, the ONLY way to drive multi-project execution from one shell is --local, which (a) loses the server's queue visibility, (b) ties the parent process to the child, and (c) negates the 'ddx agent execute-loop submits to server by default' ergonomic that execute-loop's --help promises.\n\n## Acceptance\n\n- [ ] --project flag wired through execute-loop/execute-bead/run\n- [ ] Env var fallback documented\n- [ ] Default behavior: CWD-based project detection\n- [ ] Integration test for cross-project submission\n- [ ] execute-loop --help updated to describe project targeting","events_attachment":"ddx-0b9c980a/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-0b9c980a","issue_type":"bug","labels":["helix","area:agent","area:exec","kind:bug","gap:review-loop","tier:local"],"priority":1,"status":"closed","title":"exec: execute-loop --project flag to target a specific project when server is multi-project","updated_at":"2026-04-15T06:05:45.856928108Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:101:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer running `go test`, my test runs do not leave behind registered projects in my real `~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json`. When a machine has already been polluted, I can prune it with one command. The server sweep is aggressive enough that obvious test-dir pollution does not survive a restart.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Fix A — test isolation enforced by CI.**\n   - Every existing test that instantiates `ServerState` or boots a server helper is migrated to an isolated state dir. No test writes to `~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json`.\n   - A lint test (Go or shell) parses `cli/internal/server/**/*_test.go` (and any other directories that wire up `ServerState`) and fails if it finds a construction that does not pass an explicit isolated state path or override `XDG_DATA_HOME` first. Test cases added for both the allowed-pattern and the disallowed-pattern.\n\n2. **Fix B — sweep recognizes test-dir pollution.**\n   - `ServerState.migrate` drops entries whose `Path` matches any of:\n     - prefix `/tmp/`, `/private/tmp/`, `/var/folders/`\n     - regex `/Test[A-Z][A-Za-z0-9_]*\\d+/` anywhere in the path\n   - Missing paths matching these patterns are dropped on first sweep (no 24h tombstone).\n   - Reachable paths matching these patterns are still dropped (they are never real projects — the test dir may exist transiently but is never the user's registered project).\n   - Unit test seeds a fixture state with one real project, one `/tmp/TestFoo123/001` missing, one `/tmp/TestBar456/002` still on disk, and one `/private/tmp/TestMac789/001`; asserts only the real project survives.\n\n3. **Fix C — pruning CLI.**\n   - `ddx server state prune [--dry-run] [--state \u003cpath\u003e]` applies the Fix B rules to an on-disk state file.\n   - Writes `server-state.json.bak-\u003cYYYYMMDDTHHMMSS\u003e` before overwriting.\n   - Prints summary: `Pruned X of Y entries (Z kept)`. Dry-run prints the summary without writing.\n   - Integration test uses a fixture state containing 10 real + 100 fake entries, runs the command, asserts 10 remain and a backup file with exactly 110 entries was written.\n\n4. **Perf amplifier — regression test.**\n   - A test asserts `GetBeadSnapshots` does **not** call `os.Stat` on any project already tombstoned or pattern-filtered. (Implementation detail: sweep on startup + skip tombstoned in the hot loop.)\n   - If Fix A+B are in place, a fresh `go test ./...` run followed by a server restart produces a state file with ≤ 20 projects on a typical dev machine. Asserted via a CI job that runs the tests, starts the server briefly, inspects the state file, and fails if test-dir patterns are present.\n\n5. **Backward compatibility.**\n   - The prune CLI is safe to run on an existing polluted state file. The sweep upgrade is safe on restart — no data loss for real projects.\n   - On upgrade, a note is logged: \"Pruned X phantom test-dir projects from state file.\"\n\n6. **Cross-reference.**\n   - Bead notes link to `ddx-9ce6842a` (beads perf) and `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions feed) as the two surfaces whose perf problems are amplified by this bug. Those beads' fixture sizing assumptions were correct for a clean state file; the operator experience will still feel slow on a polluted machine until Fix C is run.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"a101e215a0b34590951f51340d2f25c07ff1c5cc","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:44:13.806406362Z","description":"## Observed\n\n`~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json` on a developer machine contains **7,343 registered projects**. Composition:\n\n- **6,936 named \"001\"** — all are Go test temp dirs like `/tmp/TestAgentCapabilitiesCommandJSON1451829170/001`, `/tmp/TestAgentCheckSuccess3666443068/001`.\n- **344 named \"002\"** — same pattern in tests that use `002/`.\n- **~60 real projects** (ddx, helix, axon, plus per-branch worktrees).\n- Only 135 of the 6,936 are marked unreachable; the rest are \"alive\" because their `/tmp` dir still exists or because `LastSeen` is recent.\n- State file size: 1.8 MB.\n\n## Root cause\n\n1. Go tests that exercise the server (or anything that wires up a `ServerState`) call `RegisterProject` with a temp-dir path. The state file they write to is **not isolated** — it defaults to the shared XDG location (`serverAddrDir()` at `cli/internal/server/server.go:67`) unless the test explicitly overrides `XDG_DATA_HOME` or constructs state via the `tc.StateDir` injection path.\n2. There is no test-side deregistration on teardown. Temp dirs get cleaned up by `t.TempDir()`'s own finalizer, but the project entry in the production state file stays.\n3. The reachability sweep (`ServerState.migrate` at `cli/internal/server/state.go:85-148`) tombstones missing-path entries and drops them 24h later. With thousands of entries being added per test run, the rate of addition outpaces the 24h GC.\n\n## Perf amplifier (cross-cutting)\n\nBoth `GetBeadSnapshots` (`state_graphql.go:49-105`) and `GetAgentSessionsGraphQL` (`state_graphql.go:476-489`) iterate every registered project per request. With N=7343 that's ~7k `os.Stat` + `store.ReadAll` attempts per bead-list or sessions query, mostly against dead paths. This is part of why `ddx-9ce6842a` (beads perf) and `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions feed) feel slow in practice even before their own N·M bugs are considered.\n\n## Proposed direction\n\nThree independently-landable fixes:\n\n### Fix A — Test isolation (highest leverage)\n\n- Audit every test that calls `ServerState` / `RegisterProject` / boots a server. Each one must point at an isolated state dir (`t.TempDir()` + `XDG_DATA_HOME` override, or direct `tc.StateDir` injection).\n- Add a linting test: parse `cli/internal/server/**/*_test.go` for constructions of `ServerState` or HTTP server test helpers and assert each uses an isolated state dir. Fail CI otherwise.\n- This is the real fix. The rest is damage mitigation.\n\n### Fix B — Smarter sweep (defense in depth)\n\n- Sweep: any path under `/tmp`, `/private/tmp`, `/var/folders/*/T/` (macOS test temp root), or matching `/tmp/Test*` should be treated as a **candidate test-dir project** and dropped immediately on first sweep that finds it missing — no 24h tombstone.\n- Sweep should also drop entries where `Path` contains any `Test[A-Z]\\w+\\d+` segment (the Go testing convention), regardless of whether the dir currently exists. These are never real projects.\n- Unit test: seed a state with mixed real + fake-test-dir entries, run sweep, assert only the real ones remain.\n\n### Fix C — Manual cleanup CLI\n\n- `ddx server state prune` — read state file, apply Fix B's rules, write backup to `~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json.bak-\u003ctimestamp\u003e`, write cleaned state, print summary (X dropped, Y kept).\n- Useful for recovery on machines that have already accumulated pollution — like the reporter's. Keep even after Fix A lands.\n- Dry-run flag prints the summary without writing.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Moving state to SQLite.\n- Per-user project management UI. (Follow-up if the projects list becomes a real operator surface.)\n- Renaming \"001\" away from a confusing default. The name field is just `filepath.Base(path)`; fixing Fix A makes this question moot.","events_attachment":"ddx-15f7ee0b/events.jsonl","execute-loop-last-detail":"escalation exhausted: unsupported model \"minimax/minimax-m2.7\" for harness \"gemini\"; supported models: gemini-2.5-pro, gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-flash-lite","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"feature":"FEAT-020","foo":"bar","id":"ddx-15f7ee0b","issue_type":"bug","labels":["feat-020","feat-008","testing","hygiene","perf-amplifier"],"notes":"REVIEW:BLOCK\n\n**AC 1 (Fix A):** No changes to any `*_test.go` files to isolate `ServerState` construction, and no lint test added.\n**AC 2 (Fix B):** No changes to `cli/internal/server/state.go` (`ServerState.migrate`) to add test-dir pattern matching or immediate-drop logic. No unit test with seeded fixture state.\n**AC 3 (Fix C):** No `ddx server state prune` command added in `cli/cmd/`. No backup/dry-run logic. No integration test.\n**AC 4 (Perf):** No regression test asserting `GetBeadSnapshots` skips tombstoned/filtered projects.\n**AC 5 (Compat):** No upgrade log message implemented.\n**AC 6 (Cross-ref):** No bead notes linking to `ddx-9ce6842a` or `ddx-2ceb02fa`.\nThe `result.json` reports `\"outcome\": \"task_succeeded\"` but the merged commit `af88d432` (referenced as `result_rev`) had message \"fix(server): drop phantom test-dir projects from state file\" — the actual implementation may have been merged already but is not visible in this diff. The review diff only covers `034fbf8f`, which is the evidence commit, not the implementation commit.\n\nCross-reference: This state-file pollution amplifies `ddx-9ce6842a` (beads perf) and `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions feed). Their fixture sizing assumptions are correct for a clean state file; operator experience on already-polluted machines remains slow until `ddx server state prune` is run.\n\nREVIEW:BLOCK\n\n`cli/internal/server/**/*_test.go` — no test isolation migration or CI lint test is present in the diff.\n`cli/internal/server/state.go`, `ServerState.migrate` — no test-dir pattern filtering, immediate-drop behavior, or phantom-project pruning log is present.\n`cli/internal/server/state_test.go` or equivalent — no sweep fixture test for real, missing `/tmp/Test...`, reachable `/tmp/Test...`, and `/private/tmp/Test...` projects is present.\n`cli/cmd/` or server command wiring — no `ddx server state prune [--dry-run] [--state \u003cpath\u003e]` implementation is present.\nCLI integration tests — no prune fixture test asserting 10 kept entries and a 110-entry backup is present.\n`cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go`, `GetBeadSnapshots` tests, or CI workflow files — no regression test or CI assertion for skipping tombstoned/pattern-filtered projects is present.\nBead metadata/notes — no cross-reference notes linking `ddx-9ce6842a` and `ddx-2ceb02fa` are present in the reviewed diff.","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-9b85f519","status":"closed","title":"Test runs pollute ~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json with thousands of /tmp/Test.../001 projects","updated_at":"2026-04-23T04:07:09.859726773Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:137:{"archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-15T01:13:31Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3766264","closing_commit_sha":"604151773f9b905346ee95b051e024a02f850cf0","created_at":"2026-04-15T01:11:17.497753676Z","description":"The embedded 'agent' harness can complete a task successfully (commit produced, tests pass) but then fail to terminate, getting stuck in a compaction retry loop with compaction always failing. The parent execute-loop never observes task_succeeded and hangs forever.\n\n## Observed\n\nRunning 'ddx agent execute-loop --once --local --harness agent --model qwen/qwen3-coder-next' against axon-43ed769b (axon auth-core-types bead):\n\n1. qwen3-coder-next ran the task correctly, producing commit 91d4bdb with 636 LOC of types + 109 green tests + clippy clean. The commit was present in the worktree at /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-axon-43ed769b-20260415T005652-ebe7a16a.\n\n2. Instead of terminating, the agent kept generating events. After ~20 minutes the session log was 33 MB (8461+ events, 249 messages) and stuck in:\n\n    {\"type\":\"compaction.start\",...}\n    {\"type\":\"compaction.end\",...,\"data\":{\"messages_after\":249,\"messages_before\":249,\"no_compaction\":true,\"success\":false}}\n    {\"type\":\"compaction.start\",...}\n    {\"type\":\"compaction.end\",...,\"data\":{\"messages_after\":249,\"messages_before\":249,\"no_compaction\":true,\"success\":false}}\n    ...\n\n3. Parent ddx process in __futex_wait. Had to SIGTERM the parent (PID 4141811) and manually fast-forward-merge the worktree commit to land the work.\n\n4. A1 (previous bead) ran the same harness/model and terminated correctly in ~14 min with identical-looking commit shape. The difference was bead size: A2's output was a single 633-line Rust file, vs A1's 350-line SQL + 290-line test file. A2 didn't exceed what A1 did — so the context-limit explanation doesn't fully account for it.\n\n## Expected\n\n- After the agent commits and has nothing more to do, it should emit task_succeeded and terminate\n- Compaction should either succeed (messages_after \u003c messages_before) or bail out cleanly, not retry indefinitely\n- If the context genuinely cannot be compacted, the agent should report a structured failure and preserve the commits, not spin\n\n## Scope\n\n- Detect mid-turn compaction returning success=false repeatedly and bail with a structured 'compaction_stuck' status\n- Bound the total number of compaction attempts in a single turn\n- Ensure task termination happens immediately after a successful commit + no pending tool calls\n- Add a watchdog at the execute-loop level: kill the child if no commit+progress event is observed within a budget\n\n## Evidence\n\n- Session log: /Users/erik/Projects/axon/.ddx/executions/20260415T005652-ebe7a16a/embedded/agent-1776214612561376672.jsonl (33 MB)\n- Rescued commit: 91d4bdb on axon master\n- Parent process: PID 4141811 (killed)\n\n## Acceptance\n\n- [ ] Compaction retry loop is bounded and fails structurally\n- [ ] Post-commit termination path is deterministic\n- [ ] Watchdog detects and terminates stuck sessions with a preserved-commit outcome\n- [ ] Regression test reproduces the stuck state (seeded session with large no-op tool calls)","events_attachment":"ddx-25a41f51/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-15T01:39:31.55993889Z","id":"ddx-25a41f51","issue_type":"bug","labels":["helix","area:agent","area:exec","kind:bug","gap:stability"],"owner":"ddx","priority":0,"session_id":"eb-e80e780a","status":"closed","title":"bug: embedded agent stalls in post-commit compaction loop","updated_at":"2026-04-15T03:46:00.206978601Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:145:{"acceptance":"- [ ] Option A, B, or C selected and documented in an ADR or design note under the ddx repo\n- [ ] Implementation lands with migration path for existing .ddx/beads.jsonl files (no lost bead history)\n- [ ] Concurrent writes from 2+ workers on the same machine produce a deterministic, semantically correct tracker state\n- [ ] Concurrent writes from 2+ machines (via ddx server or shared remote) produce a deterministic, semantically correct tracker state\n- [ ] Claim races are resolved by exactly one winner; losers see a structured 'already_claimed' error\n- [ ] Crash recovery: a claimed bead whose worker died returns to unclaimed after the heartbeat timeout (existing mechanism continues to work)\n- [ ] Existing beads.jsonl format continues to work as a read-only snapshot for offline tooling (jq queries, etc.)\n- [ ] Unit + integration tests covering the concurrency scenarios above\n- [ ] Migration test: apply the upgrade to axon's current beads.jsonl (748+ beads on sindri side, 30+ new on eitri side) and verify no semantic loss","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:20:46.447948869Z","description":"Fourth child of ddx-b7ee0d6f. .ddx/beads.jsonl is a git-tracked mutable ledger. Multiple writers on different machines or in parallel worker processes produce divergent files that require manual textual merge. git merge can sometimes append-merge JSONL cleanly if each side touches disjoint lines, but when both sides touch the same bead (claim / close / add-event), the merge is semantically broken even when git reports it as resolved.\n\n## Evidence\n\nOn axon, eitri and sindri both wrote to beads.jsonl independently for 14 days. Local has 134 commits touching the tracker; origin has 336. When I tried to rebase my planning commit onto origin, git reported conflicts in .ddx/beads.jsonl — and these conflicts would have been 'resolved' by git's textual merge logic even when the semantic outcome is wrong (e.g. two machines claiming the same bead with different claimed-machine values).\n\nThe existing execute-loop help text says:\n\n    By default execute-loop submits to the running ddx server as a background\n    worker and returns immediately. Use --local to run inline in the current\n    process.\n\nSo a ddx server EXISTS and is supposed to coordinate. But .ddx/beads.jsonl is still the ledger, which means the server is not the source of truth — it's just a submission surface. The git-tracked file is authoritative, and that's what's racing.\n\n## Options (pick one during implementation)\n\n**A. Server-authoritative tracker.** When the ddx server is running, it owns beads.jsonl. All reads and writes go through the server's API. The git-tracked file becomes a periodic snapshot for backup/offline use, not the source of truth. Workers on multiple machines talk to a shared server.\n\n**B. CRDT append-only log.** Replace the current representation with an append-only log keyed by (machine_id, bead_id, sequence, timestamp). Each writer appends to its own partition; readers compute the effective state by merging partitions with a conflict-resolution rule (last-writer-wins per (bead_id, field), or better). git textual merge of the partitioned log is always safe because writers never share lines.\n\n**C. Per-bead lock files with atomic rename.** Each bead mutation acquires a lock file (beads/\u003cid\u003e.lock), writes beads/\u003cid\u003e.json atomically via rename, releases the lock. The JSONL aggregate is regenerated from per-bead files. Git tracks per-bead files, not the aggregate. Safe across local parallelism but not across machines without network coordination.\n\n**Recommendation**: A is the cleanest if the ddx server is already deployed as authoritative infrastructure. B is the best fit for the current decentralized model. C is a stopgap that solves intra-machine parallelism but not multi-machine drift.\n\n## Scope note\n\nDo NOT conflate this bead with the fetch/push/rebase triad (ddx-bd42f640, ddx-83ec1430, ddx-6aa50e57). Those three fix the git-level divergence; this bead fixes the tracker-level divergence. Both are needed — the triad without tracker safety would still produce semantically-conflicting beads.jsonl merges even when the underlying git history is linear.\n\n## Test cases\n\n- Two workers on the same machine claim different beads concurrently: both claims persist correctly\n- Two workers on different machines claim different beads concurrently: both claims persist correctly (requires network coordination)\n- Two workers attempt to claim the same bead: one wins, the other sees 'already claimed' and picks a different one\n- Recovery from crash between claim and CloseWithEvidence: the bead returns to unclaimed after a heartbeat timeout\n- Tracker state is deterministic from any valid ordering of concurrent writes (CRDT option)","id":"ddx-2791dc4f","issue_type":"bug","labels":["bug","execute-loop","tracker","concurrency"],"parent":"ddx-b7ee0d6f","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-820c201","status":"closed","title":"execute-loop: .ddx/beads.jsonl concurrent-write safety","updated_at":"2026-04-14T14:45:05.880884507Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:160:{"acceptance":"AC1. ProviderTimeout is configurable per-project via .ddx/config.yaml under agent.routing or agent.endpoints — e.g. agent.endpoints[provider].request_timeout_seconds — and the current 15-minute hardcoded constant becomes a default that can be overridden upward (or removed entirely) per provider.\n\nAC2. ProviderTimeout is configurable per-tier via the model-overrides path: cheap tier on a known thinking model (qwen3.6, deepseek-r1, etc.) defaults to a longer cap (e.g. 60 min or unlimited). Cloud chat models keep the 15-min default.\n\nAC3. The idle-read timeout (DefaultProviderIdleReadTimeout = 5 min) is the primary stalled-TCP-socket defense; documentation in cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go and the user-facing config docs explain the relationship between idle-read and wall-clock-request timeouts and when each fires.\n\nAC4. When ProviderTimeout fires, the error message names the exact timeout setting and the path to override it (e.g. 'request_timeout=15m exceeded; configure agent.endpoints.\u003cname\u003e.request_timeout_seconds in .ddx/config.yaml').\n\nAC5. execute-loop / execute-bead --request-timeout DURATION CLI flag exists for one-off overrides during debugging.\n\nAC6. Default for known-thinking-model surfaces (provider.type=lmstudio AND model name matches qwen3.6/qwen-r1/deepseek-r1 etc., or some other declared 'thinking' attribute) is bumped to a sensible value (e.g. 60 min) without requiring per-project config.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-29T20:58:18Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"932496","closing_commit_sha":"99375eaf330ea13838d40d2176d193ae3cd3453b","created_at":"2026-04-29T18:50:46.475563514Z","description":"Source: cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go:30\n  const DefaultProviderRequestTimeout = 15 * time.Minute\n\nThis is hardcoded, not surfaced through .ddx/config.yaml or any flag, and is applied uniformly to every provider regardless of model class. It is set on every service path:\n  - cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:116  ProviderTimeout: DefaultProviderRequestTimeout\n  - cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:153           ProviderTimeout: DefaultProviderRequestTimeout\n\nThe comment justifies it as 'Defeats RC4 of ddx-0a651925: a stalled TCP socket that has delivered headers but stopped emitting body bytes would otherwise pin a goroutine until the outer wall-clock (3h) frees it.'\n\nBut that scenario is already handled by a separate idle-read timeout immediately below, on line 34:\n  const DefaultProviderIdleReadTimeout = 5 * time.Minute\n\nThe 5-minute idle-read timeout is exactly the right tool for the stalled-TCP-socket case (no body bytes for 5 min → fail). The 15-minute wall-clock cap on a single request is the WRONG tool — it kills any legitimately long single Chat call regardless of streaming health.\n\nConcrete impact observed today (2026-04-29 axon repo):\n\n1. axon-db7a6d0a worker (qwen3.6-35b-a3b on bragi via lmstudio)\n   - status: no_changes\n   - duration_ms: 1,762,081 (29 min total — one timeout + retry)\n   - log: 'WARN provider error, retrying attempt=1 err=\"provider request timeout: wall-clock 15m0s\" delay=1s'\n   - Retry consumed another full 15 min, also timed out, no commits produced.\n\n2. axon-80979cb8 + axon-db7a6d0a + axon-ab2e52e0 (3-worker cascade re-launch)\n   - All 3 hit 'provider request timeout: wall-clock 15m0s' on the first attempt\n   - User killed the run before retries could waste another 15 min × 3 each\n\nWhy thinking models hit this:\nqwen3.6-35b-a3b and similar 'thinking' / chain-of-thought reasoning models can spend 5–10+ minutes generating reasoning tokens before producing the first body delta the harness considers progress. Streaming output IS happening (so the idle-read timeout would NOT fire), but the harness is counting wall-clock against the entire request. A single hard problem can blow the 15-minute cap legitimately.\n\nWhy arbitrary timeouts are not helping:\n\na) Same value for cloud providers (claude, codex) and local providers (lmstudio, omlx). Local thinking models routinely need \u003e15 min on hard prompts; claude rarely does. Same cap is wrong for both classes.\n\nb) No way to opt out of the cap per provider, per model, per request, per project. /home/erik/Projects/axon/.ddx/config.yaml has no knob.\n\nc) Retries inherit the same cap. A timeout-prone session retries with the same fate. Burns cycles and tokens.\n\nd) The cap defeats the cascade: when a cheap-tier qwen times out, escalation to standard claude is intended — but on a thinking model the timeout doesn't mean 'broken provider', it means 'this prompt is hard'. We then escalate after wasting 15 min, and the standard tier solves the problem in 2. Net effect of cheap-tier-first becomes negative.","events_attachment":"ddx-2c63bb95/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-29T21:15:18.844737462Z","id":"ddx-2c63bb95","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:routing","area:harness","kind:design-flaw"],"owner":"erik","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-2db5279a","status":"closed","title":"agent: hardcoded 15-minute DefaultProviderRequestTimeout kills thinking-model requests; not configurable","updated_at":"2026-04-29T21:16:13.255743379Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:179:{"acceptance":"Two execute-loop --local workers started simultaneously against the same project never execute the same bead; the second worker to attempt a claim on an already-claimed bead skips it and picks the next open one; a bead left in_progress by a crashed worker becomes reclaimable after a configurable TTL (default: 10 minutes)","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-13T20:27:14.28371455Z","description":"When two execute-loop workers scan the ready queue simultaneously, both can claim the same bead before either has committed its in_progress status, resulting in duplicated work or merge conflicts.\n\nObserved: started two execute-loop --local --harness agent workers (vidar and bragi) against the same project. Both immediately began working on the same bead in separate worktrees. Their output files were in near-perfect lockstep (131 vs 127 lines), indicating they had independently claimed and started executing the same item. One was killed manually to stop the waste.\n\nRoot cause hypothesis: the claim operation in execute-loop reads the ready queue, selects the next open bead, then writes its status to in_progress as two separate steps. If two workers interleave between the read and the write, both see the bead as open and both proceed to execute it.\n\nRequired fix: bead claiming must be an atomic compare-and-swap. The worker should attempt to transition bead status from open → in_progress using a conditional write that fails if the status is no longer open at write time (optimistic locking). If the claim fails, the worker should re-scan for the next available bead rather than proceeding.\n\nFor local workers (beads.jsonl on disk), a file-level lock around the read-claim-write cycle is sufficient. For server-submitted workers, the DDx server should serialize claims.\n\nAdditional issue: when a worker is killed mid-execution, the bead may be left in_progress indefinitely. The loop should support a claim timeout or heartbeat so abandoned in_progress beads become reclaimable after a TTL.","events_attachment":"ddx-3315dce2/events.jsonl","execute-loop-last-detail":"agent execution failed","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","execute-loop-retry-after":"2026-04-14T02:45:52Z","id":"ddx-3315dce2","issue_type":"bug","labels":["bug","execute-loop","concurrency"],"priority":1,"session_id":"eb-reconcile-c3b314f","status":"closed","title":"execute-loop: bead claiming is not atomic — concurrent workers race on the same bead","updated_at":"2026-04-14T04:25:15.697413055Z"}

The bead-event taxonomy preserved on a default-path run: kind:routing fires once, kind:cost fires once, kind:execute-bead fires once for the outcome; kind:tier-attempt and kind:escalation-summary do NOT fire on the default path (they remain reserved for --escalate)","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-29T12:28:08.165699268Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-3f406266","depends_on_id":"ddx-c7081f89","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-29T12:28:09Z"}],"description":"## Status update (filed 2026-04-29 12:50 UTC)\n\nThe bulk of this unification **already shipped** as `ddx-87fb72c2` (epic `ddx-fdd3ea36`) before this epic was filed:\n\n- `agent.routing.profile_ladders` and `agent.routing.model_overrides` are now opt-in (consulted only with `--escalate` / `--override-model`).\n- The default execute path on the CLI (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go`) calls `ResolveRoute` once per attempt instead of iterating a tier ladder.\n- `agent.routing.default_harness` was removed (hard error if still present).\n- `agent.ResolveTierCandidates` (from `ddx-5538aa5b`) replaced the broken literal-tier-name-as-Model pattern; tier resolution now consults the catalog.\n- See `docs/migrations/routing-config.md` for the migration the user-facing CLI surface follows.\n\n**Remaining gap (this epic now scopes only this):** the server worker path in `cli/internal/server/workers.go:650` was not updated by `ddx-87fb72c2`. It still derives `escalationEnabled := spec.Harness == \"\" \u0026\u0026 spec.Model == \"\"` instead of mirroring the CLI's `escalate \u0026\u0026 harness == \"\" \u0026\u0026 model == \"\"`. That mismatch is why `ddx work` (default mode, server-submit) still hits \"all tiers exhausted — no viable provider\" with `model_overrides` pointing at non-served models, while `ddx work --local --no-adaptive-min-tier` succeeds. Verified live in this session: `worker-20260429T033325-bedf` failed; `--no-adaptive-min-tier` got `ddx-98e6e9ef` to merge at `115b47c0` on local-tier qwen for zero cost.\n\nSole child: `ddx-c7081f89`. Once that lands, this epic closes.\n\n## Strategic direction (preserved for future reference)\n\nDDx embeds the agent SDK as a Go library to produce traceable execution units: `(bead, input_state) → (output_state, processing_metadata)`. DDx owns the execution unit and its metadata (bead events, cost ledger, escalation summary, evidence directories), and it owns operator policy (`--harness`/`--model`/`--profile`/`--max-cost`/`--no-review`/`--escalate`). It does **not** need to own provider/harness/cost/local-first routing — agent SDK already does that.\n\n| Concern | DDx (now, post-87fb72c2) | Agent SDK |\n|---|---|---|\n| Profile selection | `RouteRequest.Profile` straight to agent | `default/cheap/standard/smart/local/offline/air-gapped/fast/code-smart/code-high` |\n| Per-tier model pinning | opt-in via `--override-model` | `RouteRequest.Model` |\n| Escalation on no-provider | opt-in via `--escalate` (CLI path); **not yet on server worker path** | `escalateProfileLadder` walks `ProfileEscalationLadder=[cheap,standard,smart]` on `ErrNoLiveProvider` |\n| Cost-aware ranking | n/a (delegated) | `internal/routing/score.go` ranks by `CostClass` (local=0 wins) |\n| Adaptive success-rate gating | `escalation.AdaptiveMinTier` (sticky 50-window cheap-tier lockout, only consumed via `--escalate`) | `routing.Inputs.HistoricalSuccess map[harness]float64` |\n| Decision trace | bead events `kind:tier-attempt` per RouteCandidate | `RouteDecision.Candidates []RouteCandidate` |\n\n## Optional follow-ups (not in this epic — file separately if/when needed)\n\nThese are no longer blockers; track separately if pursued:\n\n- **Default profile = `local`** so the operator-default honors the local-LLM-first goal without an explicit `--profile local`. Currently default is `default` which maps via project config.\n- **Inject `routing.Inputs.HistoricalSuccess` per-harness** to replace `escalation.AdaptiveMinTier` with agent-native success-rate routing. Frees DDx from maintaining its own success-window state machine.\n- **Delete `cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go` helpers** once no caller (CLI or server) consumes them. Currently consumed only via `--escalate`; if `--escalate` is dropped or moved into agent, these can go.\n- **Delete `cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go::AdaptiveMinTier`** once the HistoricalSuccess injection above is in place.\n\n## Out of scope for this epic\n\n- Changes to agent SDK source — if a gap is found, file an upstream bead under `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/`.\n- UI/e2e work — the parent e2e drift epic `ddx-ccdf9cf9` covers that and is independent.\n- Server source changes outside `cli/internal/server/workers.go` — no API endpoint changes, no GraphQL schema changes.","events_attachment":"ddx-3f406266/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-3f406266","issue_type":"epic","labels":["area:routing","area:agent","kind:refactor","phase:build"],"notes":"REVIEW:BLOCK\n\nDiff contains only execution evidence files (manifest.json, result.json) — no source changes to cli/internal/server/workers.go or anywhere else. None of the AC items can be verified from this diff.","priority":1,"status":"closed","title":"routing-unify: delegate provider/harness/cost/local-first routing to agent SDK; ddx preserves execution-unit metadata + operator policy","updated_at":"2026-04-29T18:30:06.928832371Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:229:{"acceptance":"- [ ] New command or subcommand: `ddx doctor divergence` or `ddx divergence check` prints the divergence report\n- [ ] Thresholds are configurable via .ddx/config.yaml (default: 5 commits each side, 7 days merge-base age)\n- [ ] execute-loop refuses to start in ERROR state unless --force passed\n- [ ] Structured event 'divergence_status' emitted at loop start and on every doctor run\n- [ ] Optional webhook integration via config.yaml alert_webhook_url\n- [ ] Unit tests: each state (clean, ahead, behind, diverged, network-down, drift)\n- [ ] Integration test: run the loop on a diverged repo and observe the refuse-to-start behavior\n- [ ] Docs: README section on divergence monitoring, thresholds, and how to recover","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:21:10.525089812Z","description":"Fifth child of ddx-b7ee0d6f. The 14-day silent divergence on axon (eitri local 134 commits ahead of merge-base, sindri/origin 336 commits ahead) was caught only because a human happened to try pushing. A trivial monitor running a few times a day would have flagged the problem at 5 commits, not 470.\n\n## Required behavior\n\nAdd a lightweight divergence check that runs either:\n- At the top of each execute-loop iteration (cheap, ~50ms), OR\n- Periodically via a scheduled task (ddx doctor or similar), OR\n- Both\n\nThe check computes:\n- `git fetch origin \u003cbranch\u003e` (tolerant of network failure)\n- local_ahead = `git rev-list --count origin/\u003cbranch\u003e..HEAD`\n- origin_ahead = `git rev-list --count HEAD..origin/\u003cbranch\u003e`\n- merge_base_age = `git log -1 --format=%ar $(git merge-base HEAD origin/\u003cbranch\u003e)`\n\nEmit a structured event 'divergence_status' with these fields. If:\n- local_ahead \u003e threshold (default 5): warn\n- origin_ahead \u003e threshold (default 5): warn\n- both \u003e 0: ERROR — diverged, neither side is a strict ancestor\n- merge_base_age \u003e threshold (default 7 days): warn (drift)\n\nIntegration surface:\n- `ddx doctor` prints the divergence report\n- `ddx agent execute-loop` refuses to start if in the ERROR state (both sides ahead) unless --force is passed\n- An optional webhook / notification channel (config: alert_webhook_url) for ops teams\n\n## Why this is P1 not P0\n\nThe fetch/push/rebase triad (P0) prevents NEW divergence. This bead catches EXISTING divergence and alerts before it grows. Without the P0 fixes, this alert would fire constantly; with them, it should fire rarely. Still valuable as defense in depth.\n\n## Test cases\n\n- Fresh clone: report is clean (0 ahead / 0 behind)\n- Local 3 commits ahead: report shows local_ahead=3, no warning\n- Local 6 commits ahead: report shows local_ahead=6, warning emitted\n- Both sides ahead: ERROR status, execute-loop refuses to start\n- Merge base 10 days old with both sides at 0: drift warning, execute-loop continues\n- Network unreachable: report shows 'unknown', no ERROR, execute-loop continues","id":"ddx-42dd982c","issue_type":"task","labels":["execute-loop","monitoring","observability"],"parent":"ddx-b7ee0d6f","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-d16c6e3","status":"closed","title":"execute-loop: divergence monitoring and alerting","updated_at":"2026-04-14T15:56:12.030389438Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:258:{"acceptance":"The epic stays open indefinitely as a backlog for cost-tiered execution and measurement work. Closes ONLY if DDx pivots away from this optimization goal entirely (unlikely). Progress is measured by: (a) count of child beads closed that concretely improved the ladder, reviewed-rejection rate, or measurement granularity; (b) observable reduction in cost-per-closed-bead over time once the metric exists; (c) observable reduction in opus-review rejection rate as the cheap-tier work improves.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"c4518bd8b520a204440559044aeecaa799fcf414","created_at":"2026-04-15T03:47:42.980708133Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-4b59b816","depends_on_id":"ddx-e7254128","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-20T13:58:09Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-4b59b816","depends_on_id":"ddx-7bc0c8d5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-20T14:36:21Z"}],"description":"Standing optimization goal captured 2026-04-15: get the most work done for the least cost, and continuously improve our ability to measure and tune that trade-off.\n\n## Operating principles\n\n1. Cheap models do the work. Local (vidar-coder / qwen3-coder-next / other LM Studio hosts), then budget-tier cloud (claude haiku / gpt-5.4-mini), then standard (claude sonnet / gpt-5.4), then smart (claude opus / gpt-5.4 high-effort). Start cheap; escalate on failure.\n2. Strong models review. Every closed bead gets an opus review against its acceptance. Rejected reviews reopen with specific fixes.\n3. Deterministic fixes are the top rung of the ladder. When LLMs keep failing on a mechanical task, apply the fix manually; capture as input for routing policy.\n4. Measurement feeds optimization. Track per-bead which model attempted, how long, whether it passed review, what failure mode if any.\n\n## Existing bead tie-ins (helix review-loop epic from parallel agent)\n\n- ddx-a317208c exec: post-merge review agent on-by-default in execute-loop\n- ddx-dc231144 exec: tier-based auto-escalation driven by resource discovery\n- ddx-fdf031c9 exec: ddx bead review \u003cid\u003e — template a review prompt\n- ddx-44084b3e exec: ddx bead reopen \u003cid\u003e — atomic review-driven reopen\n- ddx-518b2690 exec: ddx agent run --output text — plain result extraction\n\n## Existing bead tie-ins (mine)\n\n- ddx-bd0691fe Epic: surface ddx-agent routing visibility (providers/models/check/route-status commands — prereq for routing policy)\n- ddx-bfc1aeaf Visibility 8: capture routing decisions in bead evidence (kind:routing) — feeds the measurement database\n\n## New child beads (to be filed under this epic)\n\nThe beads below are scope for this epic. File as children when we are ready to work each one. NOT pre-filed to avoid blocking the current Svelte+GraphQL migration.\n\n1. Per-bead cost aggregation: extend bead evidence schema to record tokens-in/tokens-out/cost-usd per attempt; add ddx bead metrics aggregate command that rolls up per-bead totals.\n2. Ladder success-rate analytics: ddx agent metrics tier-success — query last N closed beads, report success rate at each tier (vidar-coder, claude-sonnet, claude-opus, deterministic-fix).\n3. Prompt-context pre-filter: shrink the prompt for cheap-tier attempts — omit the full plan doc and include only the bead description + acceptance + 2 most relevant files.\n4. Failure-mode taxonomy: document the known failure modes (vidar long-context hang, read-loop stall, ship-unbuilt-code, opus-shape-miss, etc.) as bead evidence kinds so measurement can aggregate by mode.\n5. Review-rejection telemetry: aggregate opus-review outcomes (APPROVE / APPROVE_WITH_EDITS / REJECT) per originating tier so we know where the shape gaps are.\n6. Dashboard page for the metrics (requires Svelte migration Stage 4 to land first).\n\n## Why this is captured as an epic not a memory-only goal\n\nMemory is read; epics are tracked. Future sessions should see this in ddx bead list and be able to file child beads against it as we discover new measurement needs or optimization opportunities. The memory file is the principle; this epic is the backlog.","events_attachment":"ddx-4b59b816/events.jsonl","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"id":"ddx-4b59b816","issue_type":"epic","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:optimization","area:agent","area:cli","area:measurement","goal:throughput-per-dollar","wave:deferred","workstream:agent-upgrade"],"priority":2,"session_id":"eb-2dcc8231","status":"closed","title":"Epic: cost-tiered execution + measurement — maximize closed-bead throughput per dollar","updated_at":"2026-04-28T22:37:12.898039753Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:266:{"acceptance":"AC1. When an execute-bead session exits without committing in the worktree AND without writing .ddx/executions/\u003cid\u003e/no_changes_rationale.txt, the harness records a NEW dedicated status (e.g. 'agent_silent_exit' or 'no_evidence_produced') instead of letting ddx-land-coordinator land an iteration containing only execution evidence.\n\nAC2. The codex reviewer's BLOCK rationale ('change set only adds execution metadata files') becomes redundant — the harness catches this case before the reviewer is even invoked, saving a redundant review pass and its cost.\n\nAC3. The execute-bead prompt template is updated so the rationale-on-incomplete-work instruction is harder to skip — ideally a tool the agent must invoke (e.g. SubmitNoChangesRationale tool) rather than 'write a file and exit'.\n\nAC4. When the agent did run Edit tool calls but the worktree has no commit, the harness logs a warning naming each file the agent edited that is now uncommitted, so operators can recover the work or diagnose the silent-commit-failure.\n\nAC5. Documentation in docs/ explains the agent-self-commit contract, the rationale-file fallback, and the new 'agent_silent_exit' classification.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-30T12:43:25Z","claimed-machine":"sindri","claimed-pid":"3967690","closing_commit_sha":"cedf316fc6d5082eff11bff2f38ee5618958b20f","created_at":"2026-04-30T00:10:22.985451193Z","description":"Observed today across 5 attempts on the axon repo (claude/sonnet, claude/opus, codex/gpt-5.4-mini): the agent does real work in the worktree (multiple Edit tool calls touching real source files) but never runs git commit. Result: iteration commit is built by ddx-land-coordinator with ONLY execution-evidence files (.ddx/executions/\u003cid\u003e/result.json, manifest.json, usage.json), no actual code or doc changes. The post-merge codex reviewer correctly flags BLOCK with rationale 'change set only adds execution metadata files'.\n\nConcrete evidence (axon repo, 2026-04-29):\n\n  Attempt: 20260429T235353-d14f6b91 (axon-80979cb8 / A1 sub-bead)\n  Harness: claude / model: sonnet\n  Duration: 817 sec, cost $3.61, 132 tool calls\n  Tool breakdown: 79 Bash, 34 Read, 11 Grep, 8 Edit\n  Edit targets: ui/src/lib/api.ts, ui/src/lib/policy-evaluator.ts,\n    ui/src/routes/.../policies/+page.svelte, ui/tests/e2e/link-policy-retry.spec.ts\n  Iteration commit (ddx-land-coordinator): contains exactly 2 files —\n    .ddx/executions/20260429T235353-d14f6b91/result.json (23 lines)\n    .ddx/executions/20260429T235353-d14f6b91/usage.json (9 lines)\n  Net: zero actual edits landed despite $3.61 of model time spent making them.\n\nThe same pattern appeared on:\n  20260429T235406-20eeaf2c (axon-db7a6d0a, claude/sonnet, $0.57, 4.2 min, BLOCK)\n  20260429T190657-fa91f49b (axon-ab2e52e0, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.08, 24s, stalled)\n  20260429T190634-579df880 (axon-80979cb8, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.12, 104s, BLOCK)\n  20260429T190646-5aa5b46e (axon-db7a6d0a, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.06, 25s, BLOCK)\n\nTotal burn today: ~$4.50 across 5 attempts on 3 beads, 0 closed.\n\nThe execute-bead prompt template tells the agent to commit:\n  '4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with git add \u003cspecific-paths\u003e...'\n\nSo agent self-commit is the contract. But:\n\n(a) When typecheck/lint/test fails, the agent is told to fix or write no_changes_rationale.txt — but apparently many agents are bailing silently without writing the rationale either, so the harness sees a clean worktree (no diff staged or committed) and treats it as 'agent made no commits'. The iteration then carries only the auto-generated execution evidence.\n\n(b) There may also be a path where the agent commits but a pre-commit hook silently rejects it. Have not confirmed; the worktree was reaped before I could inspect.\n\nThe issue spans multiple harnesses (claude, codex via embedded openrouter), so it is not specific to one provider's commit semantics. Either:\n  - The harness should reject 'agent made no commits AND no no_changes_rationale.txt' as a separate failure class instead of silently letting ddx-land-coordinator land an empty iteration\n  - The agent prompt should make rationale-writing on incomplete work non-optional with stronger language\n  - The harness should run a final-stage check 'did the agent produce ANY signal — commit OR rationale — and if not, treat as harness-execution-failed and surface to the operator'","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-30T12:43:25.451101407Z","id":"ddx-4e0a4a59","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:harness","area:execute-bead","kind:correctness"],"owner":"erik","priority":1,"status":"closed","title":"execute-bead: agents make edits but skip git commit; iteration commits land only execution evidence","updated_at":"2026-04-30T12:43:46.370829758Z"}
