.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:275:{"acceptance":"- [ ] --output flag accepts text | json-result | session-jsonl\n- [ ] Default is text\n- [ ] Integration test covers all three modes\n- [ ] No behavior change for session-jsonl mode","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-15T05:26:10Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3766264","closing_commit_sha":"cbaee23432d7fe8167b5dd1974fc237dc20437a4","created_at":"2026-04-15T00:49:48.719835899Z","description":"ddx agent run currently prints the agent's full session (JSONL events) to stdout regardless of flags. To extract just the final assistant text message, callers have to parse JSONL and walk the session log. This breaks trivial automation like 'review=$(ddx agent run ... --prompt @/tmp/r.md)'.\n\n## Scope\n- --output text: emit only the final assistant text message to stdout (no tool calls, no JSONL)\n- --output json-result: emit only the result-type JSON object (session metadata + final text)\n- --output session-jsonl: current behavior; keep as non-default opt-in\n- Default switches to --output text\n\n## Context\nObserved during axon ADR-018 loop: both review runs spilled 90-200KB of session JSONL to stdout, requiring python3 JSON-walking to extract the ~2KB opus verdict. --output text would have made the review invocation one line instead of three.","events_attachment":"ddx-518b2690/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-15T05:36:40.703736961Z","id":"ddx-518b2690","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","area:agent","area:cli","kind:feature","gap:review-loop"],"owner":"ddx","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-dca40d97","status":"closed","title":"exec: ddx agent run --output text — plain result extraction","updated_at":"2026-04-15T05:36:54.864845558Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:280:{"archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-30T03:39:31.542862754Z","description":"Finding from fresh-eyes review of the run/try/work boundary plan.\n\nProblem:\nThe plan says there should be one simple run command layered under try and work, but current governing docs and shipped skill references still describe ddx agent run, ddx agent execute-bead, execute-loop, quorum harness flags, and agent-provider command surfaces as active user/automation primitives. Examples found during review include FEAT-006 Agent Service overview/responsibilities, FEAT-010 Layer 1 wording, and cli/internal/skills/ddx/reference/agents.md persona/quorum/help examples. Parallel copied skill docs under .agents/skills/ddx and .claude/skills/ddx contain the same stale vocabulary.\n\nWhy this matters:\nThe docs are executable guidance for agents. Leaving legacy command names and flags in skills will keep agents routing work through the old surfaces even if the implementation adds top-level run/try/work.\n\nRequired fix:\nMake the public command taxonomy bright-line and uniform across feature docs, skill docs, and CLI help: ddx run is the single agent execution primitive, ddx try layers retry/review policy over run, and ddx work layers bead orchestration over try. Agent-internal service commands may exist only as hidden/internal plumbing and must not be documented as workflow commands.\n\nAcceptance:\n- FEAT-006 and FEAT-010 no longer describe ddx agent run/execute-bead/execute-loop as public orchestration commands.\n- DDx skill references in cli/internal/skills/ddx, .agents/skills/ddx, and .claude/skills/ddx use ddx run/try/work consistently.\n- Quorum/compare/persona examples are either migrated to the simplified surface or removed if they imply DDx harness routing.\n- A docs/skill grep test or validation step fails on stale public references to ddx agent run, execute-bead, execute-loop, --harnesses, or provider-routing examples outside explicit migration notes.","id":"ddx-52ce6249","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","area:docs","area:cli"],"parent":"ddx-deb9be7e","priority":1,"spec_id":"review:power-passthrough-boundary-stale-commands","status":"closed","title":"review finding: remove stale ddx agent run and execute-bead command guidance","updated_at":"2026-04-30T03:50:03.186901956Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:286:{"acceptance":"Concurrent execute-bead workers no longer checkpoint unrelated DDx runtime churn from other active attempts into their base revisions; the snapshot boundary for DDx-owned files is documented and enforced; and concurrency tests cover the reproduced multi-worker case.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"85513f47896eaa63d7a9feaeda4318867131d6aa","created_at":"2026-04-11T04:05:59.944262787Z","description":"\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\nObserved while running multiple execute-loop workers concurrently on 2026-04-11. Replacing git stash with checkpoint commits fixed the immediate beads.lock/workers race, but execute-bead still starts from a dirty repo root where .ddx/beads.jsonl and other DDx-owned execution artifacts may be changing because of other live workers. That means one attempt can inherit unrelated DDx runtime/evidence churn into its checkpoint base.\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\nTighten execute-bead checkpointing so an attempt's input snapshot is not polluted by unrelated DDx-generated state from other live executions.\n\n## Scope\n- Define which DDx-owned paths are part of the execution input snapshot vs excluded runtime churn\n- Prevent unrelated live tracker/evidence/worker mutations from being captured into a new attempt base by default\n- Add concurrency-focused tests covering simultaneous workers and dirty DDx-owned state\n- Keep the tracked execution-evidence contract coherent with the new snapshot boundary","events_attachment":"ddx-55f05e07/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-55f05e07","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:correctness","area:agent","area:exec","area:git","area:cli"],"parent":"ddx-32b3008e","priority":0,"status":"closed","title":"Keep execute-bead checkpoint bases free of unrelated DDx runtime churn","updated_at":"2026-04-11T05:29:29.156293566Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:301:{"archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-15T00:57:36.597150715Z","description":"'ddx agent doctor' currently reports a harness as OK if its binary exists on PATH. This is insufficient: for the embedded 'agent' harness, the actual backend is a remote inference server (LM Studio on bragi/vidar/grendel or a cloud API). A healthy binary + unreachable backend reports OK but cannot do any work.\n\n## Observed failure\n\nDuring the axon ADR-018 execute-loop, 'ddx agent doctor' reported '10/10 harnesses available' while bragi's LM Studio was stopped. Trying to run ddx-agent -provider bragi returned a 502 Bad Gateway. I had to manually curl each host to discover the real state.\n\n## Scope\n\n- Extend 'ddx agent doctor' to probe each provider in the agent config with a small 'models' or 'ping' request within a short timeout\n- Report per-provider status alongside per-harness status: provider name, host, last-probe result, last-probe ms, last-probe error\n- --quick mode: skip provider probes (current behavior), for fast checks\n- Default behavior: probe providers; show a two-column status (harness OK, provider reachable Y/N)\n- Integration with execute-loop's discovery-driven escalation (ddx-dc231144): the probe result feeds the per-provider cooldown map\n\n## Acceptance\n\n- [ ] doctor probes every provider in the agent config by default\n- [ ] Unreachable/5xx providers are reported as degraded, not OK\n- [ ] doctor output is machine-readable via --json\n- [ ] doctor --quick keeps the old fast-path behavior\n- [ ] Integration test: doctor against a fake provider that returns 502 reports the provider as unhealthy","events_attachment":"ddx-5bac3c6b/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-5bac3c6b","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","area:agent","area:exec","kind:bug","gap:review-loop"],"priority":1,"status":"closed","title":"exec: harness doctor must probe provider backends, not just binaries","updated_at":"2026-04-15T03:45:01.910986192Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:302:{"acceptance":"1. `complexityGate` phase wired into `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` pre-Claim. Unit test asserts: a known-decomposable bead from the historical corpus does NOT reach `Claim`; a known-atomic bead does.\n\n2. Both prompts (`library/prompts/triage/complexity-eval.md`, `library/prompts/triage/bead-split.md`) exist and pass `go test ./cli/internal/agent -run TriagePrompts`, which exercises them against the held-out eval set via the embedded agent harness.\n\n3. `complexity-eval` classifier achieves \u003e= 80% accuracy on the held-out eval slice. The corpus and eval slice are reproducibly built from `.ddx/beads.jsonl` via `scripts/triage/harvest-corpus.go`; running the script regenerates the same eval set deterministically (sorted, seeded split).\n\n4. `bead-split` produces child beads whose collective AC covers \u003e= 90% of the parent's AC tokens (string-overlap metric documented in `docs/triage/decomposition.md`), measured on the held-out eval slice.\n\n5. Recursion depth cap = 3 by default, configurable via `agent.triage.max_decomposition_depth`. At cap, parent is marked `status=blocked` with `label=needs-human-decomposition` and a `kind:triage-overflow` event is filed. Test asserts: a synthetic always-decomposable bead reaches the cap and the parent is blocked, never dispatched.\n\n6. End-to-end: run `ddx work --once` against a fixture queue containing the historical body of `agent-6c7a4c11` (or an equivalent epic). Assert: (a) gate classifies decomposable, (b) children are filed with parent link, (c) parent is not dispatched, (d) the first atomic child IS dispatched.\n\n7. Operator docs at `docs/triage/decomposition.md` cover: when the gate triggers, how to opt out per-bead (`ddx bead update \u003cid\u003e --label triage:skip`), how to configure depth cap, and how to regenerate the historical corpus.\n\n8. Tracker: parent epic has `kind:triage-decomposed` event listing child IDs; children carry `parent: \u003cid\u003e` and inherit `spec-id` from parent.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"4178ab4f6fa7dcea62b536677545c19b97822324","created_at":"2026-04-29T19:00:05.617369837Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-5bf4ee7e","depends_on_id":"ddx-26058d78","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-30T14:00:10Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-5bf4ee7e","depends_on_id":"ddx-212c5a00","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-30T14:00:10Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-5bf4ee7e","depends_on_id":"ddx-b70c71aa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-30T14:00:10Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-5bf4ee7e","depends_on_id":"ddx-ae15ab62","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-30T14:00:10Z"}],"description":"## Goal\n\nToday, when execute-loop dispatches a coarse bead (an unsplit epic) to an agent, the agent often returns `no_changes` with reasoning like \"this is an epic, needs child-bead breakdown, not monolithic execution\" — wasting a worker attempt, leaving the bead in a half-owned claimed state, and forcing manual operator intervention.\n\nConcrete recent example: `agent-6c7a4c11` (Harness × Model Matrix Benchmark — initial tranche) on this user's `agent` repo was dispatched as a single bead, the agent declined to execute monolithically, the worker exited without unclaiming. The bead is now stuck claimed by a dead worker with no change applied.\n\nThe fix is to run a triage step **before** Claim: evaluate whether the bead is atomic (one PR's worth of work, single AC focus) or decomposable (multiple distinct deliverables, AC items map to separable PRs). If decomposable, run a splitter that produces child beads (parent-linked, AC-distributed), and skip dispatch of the parent. Children themselves re-enter triage — recursive decomposition with a depth cap.\n\n## Concrete change\n\nInsert a `complexityGate` phase in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` between bead-pick and Claim:\n\n```\nnext-candidate → ResolveRoute → preflight gate (ddx-98e6e9ef)\n  → complexityGate (NEW)\n  → if atomic:        Claim → invoke\n  → if decomposable:  splitter → file children with parent link → mark parent blocked-by-children → re-pick\n  → if ambiguous:     surface to human triage (event + label), don't claim\n```\n\nTwo prompts, both shipped with the default DDx plugin under `library/prompts/triage/`:\n\n1. **`complexity-eval.md`** — given bead body + AC + deps + history (prior attempts, reviewer BLOCKs), classify `{atomic, decomposable, ambiguous}` with reasoning + confidence.\n2. **`bead-split.md`** — given a decomposable bead, emit N child beads (JSON) with: title, AC, parent link, spec-id inheritance, in-scope files subset, labels. Children must collectively cover the parent's AC.\n\nBoth prompts use the embedded `agent` harness with profile=default (cheap tier, local-first) — the gate's cost/latency must be \u003c\u003c a wasted dispatch.\n\n## Historical evidence engineering\n\nBoth prompts must be engineered against historical evidence, not vibes. Harvest a corpus from `.ddx/beads.jsonl` (this project + the user's `agent` project, which has 5 months of bead history including known epic-rejection cases):\n\n- Beads closed `no_changes` whose status text mentions \"epic\", \"split\", \"breakdown\", \"scope\", or \"monolithic\" → positive examples for **decomposable**.\n- Beads with `attempt_count \u003e= 3` or repeated `kind:reopen` events from reviewer BLOCKs citing scope → positive examples for **decomposable**.\n- Beads that were eventually split manually (parent epic + N child tasks filed in a single tracker batch) → use parent body as splitter input, the actual children as ground-truth output.\n- Beads closed cleanly on first attempt with single-file or small-diff commits → positive examples for **atomic**.\n\nHold out 20% as eval. Report `complexity-eval` accuracy/F1 on classification; report `bead-split` AC-coverage rate (children's AC tokens together cover parent's AC) and child-count distribution vs. ground-truth.\n\nThe harvest script (`scripts/triage/harvest-corpus.go` or equivalent) is committed and reproducible.\n\n## Recursion + depth cap\n\nChildren re-enter the gate. Default depth cap = 3, configurable via `agent.triage.max_decomposition_depth` in `.ddx/config.yaml`. At cap: surface to human (`kind:triage-overflow` event, `status=blocked`, `label=needs-human-decomposition`). Never silently dispatch a parent that hit cap.\n\n## In-scope files\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` — new `complexityGate` phase pre-Claim\n- `cli/internal/agent/triage.go` (new) — gate logic, prompt invocation, child filing\n- `library/prompts/triage/complexity-eval.md` (new)\n- `library/prompts/triage/bead-split.md` (new)\n- `cli/internal/agent/triage_test.go` (new) — unit tests against held-out historical corpus\n- `library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl` (new) — frozen eval slice (training slice can be regenerated)\n- `scripts/triage/harvest-corpus.go` (new) — corpus regeneration\n- `docs/triage/decomposition.md` (new) — operator docs\n\n## Out of scope (file follow-ups if/when needed)\n\n- Backfill triage on already-claimed in-flight beads (e.g. unsticking agent-6c7a4c11 retroactively) — operational bead, not part of this feature.\n- A web UI for visualizing the triage decision per bead — consumer bead.\n- Per-team customization of split rules — premature; ship the default and iterate.\n\n## Anti-goals\n\n- Do **not** rewrite an existing bead in place. Splitting always files children; the parent stays as an epic with `status=blocked-by-children`.\n- Do **not** make the gate silently optional. If disabled via flag or config, emit a one-time warning per worker boot.\n- Do **not** use a model larger than the cheap tier for the gate prompts.\n- Do **not** duplicate routing logic in the gate; it should be a thin caller of the agent SDK with the gate's two prompts.\n\n## Why P1\n\nThe failure mode it prevents — claimed-but-undeliverable beads — corrupts the queue's claim semantics, wastes operator time, and silently inflates `attempt_count` on epics that will never close. Without this gate, the rest of the routing-cleanup work (`ddx-fdd3ea36` umbrella) ships into a queue with structurally bad inputs.","events_attachment":"ddx-5bf4ee7e/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-5bf4ee7e","issue_type":"epic","labels":["area:dispatch","area:bead-triage","kind:feat","phase:design"],"notes":"Decomposed during queue drain into child beads ddx-26058d78 (corpus harvester), ddx-212c5a00 (prompt assets), ddx-b70c71aa (pre-claim gate wiring), and ddx-ae15ab62 (operator docs). Parent now depends on the children so it is not dispatchable as a monolithic implementation bead.","priority":1,"status":"closed","title":"pre-claim triage: complexity evaluator + auto-splitter for recursive epic decomposition before agent dispatch","updated_at":"2026-04-30T14:18:40.095401852Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:314:{"acceptance":"When `gitleaks` is unavailable, the `lefthook` fallback secret scan still catches obvious secret assignments but does not fail on ordinary `.ddx/beads.jsonl` tracker updates; the fallback inspects staged additions rather than whole files or otherwise avoids re-flagging historical tracker content; and a deterministic repro covers a staged tracker diff containing `token-awareness.md` text without any added secrets.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-13T15:16:12Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3842205","closing_commit_sha":"2406ae24fb21d31bf2eb2027e8d4395d4ea264fd","created_at":"2026-04-11T01:20:52.176355735Z","description":"\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\nTracker-only DDx bead commit from /Users/erik/Projects/ddx after filing execute-loop alpha findings. Environment does not have `gitleaks`, so the `lefthook` pre-commit hook used the fallback grep path in `lefthook.yml`.\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\n\nThe fallback `secrets` hook in `lefthook.yml` produces false positives on staged `.ddx/beads.jsonl` changes because it scans the entire staged file with a broad `token|auth|password|...` regex instead of scanning added lines with tighter assignment-oriented patterns.\n\n## Reproduction\n1. Ensure `gitleaks` is not installed.\n2. Stage a normal tracker-only change to `.ddx/beads.jsonl`.\n3. Run `git commit`.\n4. Observe the `secrets` hook fails and prints a line from `.ddx/beads.jsonl` where text like `token-awareness.md` and later quoted JSON content satisfy the fallback regex even though no secret was added.\n\n## Observed gap\nThe fallback pattern is broad enough to match ordinary prose containing words like `token` or `auth`, and because it scans the whole staged file it re-trips on historical tracker content whenever `.ddx/beads.jsonl` changes.\n\n## Why this matters\nIn environments without `gitleaks`, normal bead creation becomes effectively uncommittable. That blocks DDx's own tracker workflow and makes it harder to land the very bugs discovered during alpha execution.","events_attachment":"ddx-60316844/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-60316844","issue_type":"bug","labels":["helix","phase:build","area:security","area:bead","area:tooling"],"owner":"ddx","priority":0,"session_id":"eb-7019f563","spec-id":"FEAT-001","status":"closed","title":"Fix fallback secret scan false positives on tracker JSONL commits","updated_at":"2026-04-13T15:17:50.33346074Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:353:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer looking at the Sessions page, I can tell at a glance how much I actually paid in cash versus what I consumed under subscriptions versus what I ran locally. No number on the page conflates those three categories.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **`billingMode` field.** Session records (index rows + live writer) carry `billingMode` ∈ `{paid, subscription, local}`. Enum is validated on write. Unit test covers each value.\n\n2. **Classifier.** `billingModeFor(harness, surface, baseURL)` is the single source of truth. Test table includes at minimum:\n   - claude (Claude Code) → subscription\n   - codex → subscription\n   - openrouter (any model) → paid\n   - openai/anthropic with API key → paid\n   - agent (embedded) → local\n   - openai-compat endpoint pointing at localhost/127.*/192.168.*/10.* → local\n   - openai-compat endpoint pointing at a public host with an API key → paid\n\n3. **Reindex backfill.** Running `ddx agent log reindex` (from `ddx-2ceb02fa`) on a fixture with sessions from all three modes produces index rows with correct `billingMode`. No row is written without one.\n\n4. **Rollup resolver.** GraphQL exposes `sessionsCostSummary(projectId, since?, until?) { cashUsd, subscriptionEquivUsd, localSessionCount, localEstimatedUsd }`. Returns zeros/null correctly when no sessions match. Respects shard-scoped reads from `ddx-0a33bc5f`.\n\n5. **UI — three cards.** The Sessions page replaces the \"Total Cost\" card with three cards as described (Cash paid, Subscription value, Local sessions). Tooltips explain each bucket. Playwright asserts all three render and that hover on each shows the explanatory text.\n\n6. **UI — per-row badge.** Each session row's cost column shows a small `cash`/`sub`/`local` badge with a tooltip explaining the bucket. Playwright asserts badge presence and correct classification on a seeded fixture containing one session of each type.\n\n7. **Zero-state.** When the project has zero sessions of a bucket, that card shows `—` / `0`, not `$0.00`, to avoid implying a paid-zero vs absent-data collision. Playwright asserts.\n\n8. **Optional local cost config.** Setting `cost.local_per_1k_tokens` in `.ddx/config.yaml` causes the Local card to render as dollars using the configured rate × sum(totalTokens) for local sessions. Unset: count-only. Integration test covers both paths.\n\n9. **Accessibility.** The three cards and per-row badges expose their bucket meaning via `aria-label`, not just tooltip, so screen-reader users get the same clarification.\n\n10. **Cross-references.**\n    - Depends on `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions index) — the `billingMode` field must land in the index schema.\n    - Bead notes in `ddx-23978824` should reference this bead since the unified-endpoints view has overlapping concepts (what's a paid endpoint vs a subscription harness vs a local endpoint).","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"51bb2f5d3c0de00361638822249616bcc5e3e615","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:54:25.174982037Z","description":"## Observed\n\nThe Sessions page ($ sums `session.cost` into one \"Total Cost\" card, `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/.../sessions/+page.svelte:21,28,91`). The underlying `cost_usd` field is fed from three very different billing realities and the UI treats them as one:\n\n1. **Cash paid (pay-per-token).** API providers (OpenRouter, direct OpenAI, direct Anthropic API). `cost_usd` = actual billed amount.\n2. **Subscription-equivalent.** Claude Code, Codex — flat-rate subscriptions. The CLI/harness emits `total_cost_usd` as the **dollar-equivalent** for tokens consumed under the subscription (what you would have paid pay-per-token). You paid a flat fee up front; this is a value-extracted number, not a cash-out number.\n3. **Local compute.** Embedded `agent` harness, ollama/vllm endpoints. Today emits cost 0 because no one is billing; real cost is electricity + hardware amortization, not currently modeled.\n\nA user reading \"Total Cost: $X\" does not know which of those X is.\n\n## What's needed\n\nThree independent rollups, each explicitly labeled, each computable without ambiguity:\n\n1. **Cash** — sum of `cost_usd` across sessions whose billing mode is `paid`.\n2. **Subscription-equivalent** — sum across sessions whose billing mode is `subscription`. Copy: \"Dollar-equivalent for tokens consumed via Claude Code / Codex subscription. You did not pay this in cash; it's the pay-per-token rate you would have been charged.\"\n3. **Local** — either a count of sessions (if no rate is configured) or a computed dollar estimate if `local_cost_per_1k_tokens` is set in config. Default: count-only, to avoid inventing numbers.\n\n## Backing data model\n\nSession records (the sharded index from `ddx-2ceb02fa`) need a new field `billingMode: \"paid\" | \"subscription\" | \"local\"`. Derive at write time:\n\n- `paid`: harness is an API-endpoint provider (openrouter, openai-compat with an API key, anthropic API, …). Probably any harness with `Surface == \"openai-compat\"` or a known public-api provider prefix.\n- `subscription`: harness is `claude` (Claude Code) or `codex` or `gemini-cli` when using a Google account. Identify by harness name + surface.\n- `local`: harness is `agent` (embedded) OR endpoint is localhost / private IP / configured-as-local in provider config.\n\nClassification lives in one place — a small `billingModeFor(harness, surface, baseURL)` function — so the logic isn't duplicated between writer and reader.\n\n## Scope\n\n### Part 1 — Data model\n\n- Extend `SessionEntry` / the session index row with `billingMode string` (enum validated on write).\n- Add `billingModeFor(...)` helper + unit tests for each harness/surface combination.\n- Back-fill: `ddx agent log reindex` (from `ddx-2ceb02fa`) computes `billingMode` when re-shard-writing legacy sessions.\n\n### Part 2 — Backend rollup\n\n- Modify the sessions GraphQL query (or add a sibling `sessionsCostSummary(projectId, since?)`) to return `{ cashUsd, subscriptionEquivUsd, localSessionCount, localEstimatedUsd? }`.\n- Reuse the perf-safe path from `ddx-0a33bc5f` (sessions-index aggregation, shard-aware date filter).\n\n### Part 3 — UI\n\n- Replace the single \"Total Cost\" card on the Sessions page with three cards:\n  - **Cash paid** ($X.XX) — tooltip \"Billed by pay-per-token APIs (OpenRouter, direct API keys)\"\n  - **Subscription value** ($X.XX) — tooltip \"Dollar-equivalent for tokens consumed under Claude Code / Codex subscriptions. Not cash out of pocket.\"\n  - **Local sessions** (N) — tooltip \"Sessions served locally. Compute cost not modeled.\" If `local_cost_per_1k_tokens` is set, show \"$X.XX est.\" instead of a count.\n- Per-row: small badge `cash` / `sub` / `local` in the cost column, color-coded (green/blue/gray). Tooltip explains the row's bucket.\n\n### Part 4 — Optional config\n\n- `.ddx/config.yaml` gains `cost.local_per_1k_tokens: \u003cfloat\u003e`. When set, local-session total gets rendered as dollars. When unset, count-only. No auto-estimation.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Subscription flat-rate cost modeling (\"I pay $20/month for Claude Code\"). A useful adjacent bead: divide flat rate by consumption to show $/1M-tokens realized value. Not here.\n- Quota trend / headroom. .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:360:{"acceptance":"- [ ] cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go Merge function is replaced (or supplemented) with a Rebase function that takes (dir, base, head, onto) and rebases head onto onto starting from base\n- [ ] On clean rebase, master fast-forwards to the rebased tip; one commit per bead\n- [ ] On conflict, commits are preserved under refs/ddx/iterations/\u003cbead\u003e/\u003ctimestamp\u003e/ via the existing iterationRef helper, the bead is unclaimed, the worker loop stops (same shape as the fetch-divergence path)\n- [ ] The 'chore: checkpoint before merge' commit is removed entirely; a dirty worktree at landing time is a hard error with a structured message\n- [ ] No code path in the execute-loop ever calls `git merge --no-edit` or any merge strategy — verified by grep\n- [ ] LAND-LOCK REQUIREMENT: while a rebase + ff-push sequence is in progress for a given projectRoot, no other rebase + ff-push sequence may run against the same projectRoot. The serialization point can be (a) a per-project flock on .git/ddx-land.lock, (b) a single goroutine in the ddx server process that owns all rebase-land operations for that project (preferred — this matches the meta-bead ddx-8746d8a6 coordinator design), or (c) an equivalent mutex held across fetch → rebase → ff-push. Without this, two workers whose rebases complete simultaneously will both try to advance master to different SHAs and one will lose. The test for this requirement: spawn N (N\u003e=5) concurrent land calls on the same projectRoot and assert that (i) exactly one commit is on master per submission after all calls return, (ii) the submissions are processed in some total order (not a set of parallel races), (iii) any submission that loses the race is either retried against the new tip OR preserved under refs/ddx/iterations/ with a clear reason — never silently dropped\n- [ ] Unit test: two sibling workers, second rebases onto first's tip, master has exactly 2 new commits in linear order with no merge parents\n- [ ] Unit test: conflicting rebase preserves commits and emits the land_conflict status\n- [ ] Unit test: dirty worktree at landing time errors out without producing a checkpoint commit\n- [ ] Integration test: running 5 parallel workers on the same machine for 10 iterations produces exactly 50 linear commits on master with no merge parents anywhere in the reflog\n\nThis bead is subsumed by the meta-bead ddx-8746d8a6 \"execute-loop: adopt human-PR landing model end-to-end\". The coordinator pattern in the meta-bead IS the implementation of this bead's rebase + land-lock requirements. If ddx-8746d8a6 lands first, close this bead as satisfied by the coordinator's rebase step. If this bead lands first, its implementation should be the foundation for the coordinator.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:20:11.501071514Z","description":"Third child of ddx-b7ee0d6f. When two workers run in parallel on the same machine (each in its own execute-bead worktree), and both complete and attempt to land, the orchestrator uses `git merge --no-edit` with the ort strategy to fold one worker's commits into master. This produces a merge commit tree instead of a linear history, and triggers repeated 'chore: checkpoint before merge' commits when the working tree isn't clean.\n\n## Evidence (source)\n\ncli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:59-69 defines the Merge function:\n\n    // Merge merges rev into dir's working tree via git merge. It commits any...\n    func Merge(dir, rev string) error {\n        _ = osexec.Command(\"git\", \"-C\", dir, \"commit\", \"-m\", \"chore: checkpoint before merge\").Run()\n        ...\n        out, err := osexec.Command(\"git\", \"-C\", dir, \"merge\", \"--no-edit\", rev).CombinedOutput()\n        if err != nil {\n            _ = osexec.Command(\"git\", \"-C\", dir, \"merge\", \"--abort\").Run()\n            return fmt.Errorf(\"merge: %s: %w\", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)\n        }\n    }\n\n## Evidence (symptom on axon)\n\ngit reflog master on axon's eitri machine shows 12+ `Merge made by the 'ort' strategy` entries on 2026-04-13 within a 2-hour window, each one merging a sibling SHA that never appeared on origin:\n\n    master@{8}  merge 5eb67fd0afab40ba4f9b06a8228760d69187af9d\n    master@{12} merge 77999324c59bb715ee6ef290255a5b31e17b1930\n    master@{13} merge 79772765312e3bc5a0715a6d9a2860016463e594\n    master@{17} merge 268f63283af2df6bfde94200b5159222ea4f996d\n    ... and nine more\n\nInterleaved with 'chore: checkpoint before merge' commits. This is the signature of two+ parallel workers each producing sibling branches that get merged back into master one at a time, each merge preceded by a defensive checkpoint.\n\n## Required behavior\n\nReplace merge-based landing with rebase-based landing:\n\n1. When a worker completes, its worktree is at commit W (child of base B)\n2. Between B and now, master may have advanced to M (due to sibling workers or fetch-from-origin)\n3. Instead of `git merge --no-edit W` on master, do:\n   a. `git rebase --onto M B W` in the worker's worktree (or equivalent plumbing via `git cherry-pick B..W` onto M)\n   b. On conflict: preserve the worker's commits under refs/ddx/iterations/\u003cbead\u003e/\u003ctimestamp\u003e/ (existing infrastructure at execute_bead_orchestrator.go:21-26), unclaim the bead, stop the worker loop\n   c. On clean rebase: fast-forward master to the rebased tip\n4. NO checkpoint commits. If the master worktree is dirty, that's a bug in the preceding workflow (should have been committed by the worker) — fail loudly, don't paper over with a checkpoint\n\n## Why rebase over merge\n\n- Linear history — no fan of merge parents from parallel runs\n- Conflicts surface earlier (during rebase, not as a semantically-broken merge)\n- Each bead produces exactly one commit on master; a clean log of 'this bead → this commit'\n- Push-ff-only from the sibling push bead (ddx-83ec1430) works because history stays linear\n\n## Interaction with fetch bead\n\nWith ddx-bd42f640 ensuring HEAD is current with origin before work starts, and this bead ensuring intra-machine landings are linear, a worker's commit sequence is always: fetch → linear base → work → rebase → push. No merges, no sibling fans.\n\n## Remove the checkpoint-before-merge pattern\n\nThe 'chore: checkpoint before merge' commits exist because the Merge function tries to handle a dirty worktree by committing it first. In the rebase model, a dirty worktree is illegal — the worker must have committed everything before the landing phase. If the worktree is dirty at landing time, error out immediately with a clear message about the worker not having committed cleanly. Do not silently checkpoint.","id":"ddx-6aa50e57","issue_type":"bug","labels":["bug","execute-loop","git","orchestration"],"parent":"ddx-b7ee0d6f","priority":0,"session_id":"eb-43009cc","status":"closed","title":"execute-loop: rebase sibling workers instead of git merge --no-edit (ort)","updated_at":"2026-04-14T15:26:48.546705773Z"}

Keep the lifecycle enforcement (claim → implement → verify → close). Most damaging of the three report findings because it hides incomplete work.\n\nObserved on axon `axon-c5cc071a` (RBAC field/row/entity spec, ~2000-word AC) 2026-04-19:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"id\": \"axon-c5cc071a\",\n  \"claimed-at\": \"2026-04-19T23:20:45Z\",\n  \"claimed-machine\": \"sindri\",\n  \"claimed-pid\": \"3479503\",\n  \"execute-loop-heartbeat-at\": \"2026-04-19T23:20:45.364638776Z\",\n  \"updated_at\": \"2026-04-19T23:25:18.98959532Z\",\n  \"status\": \"closed\",\n  \"events\": []\n}\n```\n\n4m33s from claim to closed, `events: []`, no execution bundle under `.ddx/executions/` referencing the bead, no git commit referencing the id, no branch, no notes. Reopened 2026-04-20 as `axon-c5cc071a`. Evidence this is worker-side: no other actor claimed the bead, `claimed-pid` matches execute-loop PID pattern, `execute-loop-heartbeat-at` was set at claim — a manual `ddx bead update` would not leave `events: []` while setting `status: closed`.\n\nRoot-cause hypothesis from report: the worker's cleanup path closes the bead on primary-task success even when the reviewer sub-step errors out without returning a parseable verdict (e.g. context-overflow `n_keep: 1,868,459 \u003e= n_ctx: 196,608` seen in reporter's LLM server logs, or any exception in the review state-machine path). A `bead.result` event that never gets emitted still leaves the bead closed if the exit path is not gated on verdict presence.\n\nClosure contract should be: a bead transitions to `status=closed` only when a valid terminal review verdict (APPROVE / REQUEST_CHANGES / BLOCK / SKIP) is recorded alongside an execution bundle and at least one bead event. Worker crash, malformed reviewer output, or context-overflow during review must transition the bead back to `in_progress` with a retry marker — never to `closed`.\n\nPrior related bead `ddx-b57be3ab` (closed) addressed BLOCK-with-empty-rationale but treated the rationale-missing case as `review_malfunction` without closing; this report shows a separate path where the closure itself happens despite reviewer failure.","id":"ddx-738edf47","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:exec","area:bead","kind:bug","kind:correctness","goal:throughput-per-dollar","wave:2","workstream:reviewer"],"priority":1,"status":"closed","title":"exec: bead closes without valid reviewer verdict — silent false-closure hides unfinished work","updated_at":"2026-04-20T16:23:03.738432327Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:399:{"archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-15T06:24:35Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3766264","closing_commit_sha":"604f17d53ce26b5776472f9fa77a82a2eb1b9cba","created_at":"2026-04-15T01:35:08.058474818Z","description":"The ddx-bead skill guides bead creation but underspecifies what makes a bead *execution-ready* for automated execute-bead runs. Observed during axon ADR-018 execute-loop driving: beads that technically met the current skill's requirements (title, AC, labels, description pointing at spec-id) still produced bad execute-bead outcomes because the description lacked the context the agent needed to ship correct code in one pass.\n\n## Gaps in the current skill\n\n1. **Description quality is underspecified.** The skill gives AC quality rules but the description field just says 'per SD-004 Section 2' is OK. In practice, agents need:\n   - Exact file paths to touch (e.g., 'crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs')\n   - Cross-bead context when dependencies are listed (e.g., 'A1 landed the credential_revocations table in crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs — query it via SELECT 1')\n   - Inline spec excerpts, NOT just doc-id references. If the spec is ADR-018 §4's failure table, paste the table into the description. Don't say 'see ADR-018 §4'.\n   - Files NOT to touch (scope negation)\n\n2. **spec-id and the doc graph are unreliable for execution context.** The current skill implies spec-id linkage is sufficient. It is not — spec-id drives docgraph.BuildGraphWithConfig() which only resolves HELIX-shaped doc references. Generic beads on non-HELIX projects have no equivalent. AND even when resolution works, the agent only gets a file path, not the relevant section. The bead description is the only place that carries content the agent can rely on.\n\n3. **No guidance on cross-bead context.** When bead B depends on bead A, B's description should describe what A landed (file paths, type names, key APIs) — not assume the agent will reopen A's history. Automated execute-bead does not have that context; each bead is read cold.\n\n4. **No guidance on verification commands in AC.** AC items like 'unit tests green' are too vague. Should be 'cargo test -p axon-server green' or 'bun test passes in sdk/typescript'. Named commands.\n\n5. **No guidance on bead size.** A bead should be one unit of testable work, sized so a single agent pass can complete it. Too small is overhead; too large defeats review. Heuristic: if the AC lists more than ~12 checkboxes, consider splitting.\n\n6. **No guidance on incorporating review findings on reopen.** When a bead is reopened after a REQUEST_CHANGES review, the author should either (a) fold the review findings into the description for the next attempt to see, or (b) rely on the tool to thread review events into the prompt (a separate ddx bead — see ddx-a317208c). The skill is silent on this.\n\n7. **No template for description.** A recommended description template (Scope / Files to touch / Cross-bead context / Verification / Quality bar) would make good beads cheaper to author.\n\n## Scope\n\nUpdate skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md to add:\n\n- A 'What makes a bead execution-ready' section that codifies the points above\n- A 'Description template' with recommended sections: Scope, Files to touch, Files NOT to touch, Cross-bead context, Verification commands, Spec excerpts inline\n- A 'Bead sizing' section with the ~12-AC heuristic\n- A 'Review-driven reopen' section that tells authors to update the description in place when a bead is reopened after a review rejection\n- A 'Common anti-patterns' section enumerating the mistakes observed during the axon execute-loop:\n  - Listing doc IDs without inlining the relevant text\n  - Not naming file paths in the description\n  - AC items that are not verifiable by a named command\n  - Depending on unmerged work without explaining what it landed\n\nKeep the existing required-fields and label-conventions sections — they are correct.\n\n## Context\n\nObserved during axon ADR-018 execute-loop:\n- Bead A1 was well-sized but initially had scope creep in its description (audit_retention_policies, which belonged to F1). Agent added the scope-creep table faithfully.\n- Bead A2's initial description said 'UserIdentity { provider, subject, user_id }' — a typo carried over from an earlier ADR revision. Agent implemented 'subject' faithfully even though ADR-018 §3 said 'external_id'. The description was the contract the agent followed.\n- Bead B1 is being re-authored right now with a richer description (inline ADR-018 §4 failure table, exact file paths, cross-bead context about A1 schema and A2 types) — this is the experiment to validate the authoring changes proposed in this bead.\n\n## Acceptance\n\n- [ ] skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md gains 'What makes a bead execution-ready' section\n- [ ] Description template added with the 6 recommended sections\n- [ ] Bead sizing heuristic documented\n- [ ] Review-driven reopen guidance added\n- [ ] Common anti-patterns enumerated\n- [ ] Skill still lints against any existing skill-format CI check","events_attachment":"ddx-78613f54/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-15T06:25:35.769449241Z","id":"ddx-78613f54","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","area:bead","area:skill","kind:documentation","gap:bead-authoring"],"owner":"ddx","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-4ef43076","status":"closed","title":"skill: improve ddx-bead SKILL.md with authoring discipline for execution-ready beads","updated_at":"2026-04-15T06:25:42.623027765Z"}

(2) resolver_node.go and resolver_documents.go both use the new helpers; old encodeCursor(i int) is deleted. (3) NewResolver constructor exists, rejects nil State and empty WorkingDir with a clear error. All \"if r.State == nil\" / \"if r.WorkingDir == empty\" guards removed from individual resolvers. (4) ProjectSnapshot removed — resolver_node.go returns docgraph or server domain types directly, matching the pattern in resolver_documents.go. (5) All existing integration tests (TestGraphQLNodeInfo/TestGraphQLProjects/TestGraphQLNodeByProjectID/TestGraphQLDocuments/TestGraphQLDocGraph) still pass. (6) go build ./... clean.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-15T04:24:50Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3766264","closing_commit_sha":"0299d7701091e0153dc972dea2096258a2cae752","created_at":"2026-04-14T23:24:51.316337083Z","description":"Follow-up from Stage 2.4 opus review: the resolver pattern established in Stage 2.2 (node/projects) and extended in Stage 2.4 (documents/docGraph) has 3 load-bearing shape issues that should be fixed before Stage 2.5+ compounds them across 7 more resolver beads.\n\n1. Stable-key cursors. Current: resolver_node.go defines encodeCursor(i int) / decodeCursor(s string) that base64-wrap \"cursor:\u003cslice-index\u003e\". Slice-index cursors break when the underlying collection reorders between pages. Fine for projects (rarely mutate) but broken for beads, documents, commits, sessions, workers (mutated or resorted per query). Replace with ID-based or (sort-key, ID)-based stable cursors. New file: cli/internal/server/graphql/cursors.go with encodeStableCursor(id string) / decodeStableCursor(s string).\n\n2. Constructor-enforced non-nil state. Current: every resolver has \"if r.State == nil { return }\" or \"if r.WorkingDir == empty { return }\" early returns. As Stage 2 grows this becomes 50+ copy-pasted guards. Replace with a NewResolver(state StateProvider, workingDir string) *Resolver constructor that rejects nil/empty and returns an error, drop the runtime checks.\n\n3. Snapshot struct vs direct domain types. Current inconsistency: resolver_node.go uses ProjectSnapshot / NodeStateSnapshot (adapter structs) while resolver_documents.go uses docgraph.Document directly. Pick ONE approach and migrate the other. Recommendation: direct domain types (simpler, less boilerplate). Migrate resolver_node.go to drop ProjectSnapshot.\n\nNot in scope: the prefix-registry for node(id) dispatch (concern 5 from the Stage 2.3 note) — keep that for when bead/doc/worker prefixes are added. Not in scope: splitting StateProvider into sub-interfaces (concern 1) — decide that lazily when the interface actually grows.\n\nFile: cli/internal/server/graphql/{cursors.go,resolver.go,resolver_node.go,resolver_documents.go,state_graphql.go}","events_attachment":"ddx-7e2f2cb9/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-15T04:42:50.454228999Z","id":"ddx-7e2f2cb9","issue_type":"chore","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:refactor","area:graphql","stage:2","kind:migration"],"owner":"ddx","parent":"ddx-02d6142d","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-93fb059f","status":"closed","title":"refactor(graphql): resolver shape cleanup — stable-key cursors, constructor-enforced state, snapshot-vs-domain decision","updated_at":"2026-04-15T04:42:56.089947386Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:422:{"acceptance":"1. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... ./internal/bead/... passes after the fix.\n2. A new unit test simulates `ddx bead create` from cwd=`\u003cproject\u003e/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-XXXXX-\u003ctimestamp\u003e-\u003crandom\u003e` and asserts the new bead's id field matches `^ddx-[0-9a-f]{8}$`. Test fails on current main and passes after the fix.\n3. `grep -E '\"id\":\"\\.execute-bead-wt-' .ddx/beads.jsonl` returns 0, OR returns 12 with a recorded decision in this bead's notes that the existing 12 are deferred to a separate repair bead.\n4. A layer-2 design bead is filed (or an existing one updated) that captures the question of whether workers should create beads in-band (option c), append to the parent (option a), or surface via execute-bead result (option b). Its ID is referenced in this bead's events.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-30T00:56:04Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2278885","closing_commit_sha":"e1e5f7cfd8c9656260461a7e63d3e67883bdd77a","created_at":"2026-04-29T19:05:33.7239674Z","description":"## Two-layer problem\n\n### Layer 1 (mechanical bug)\n\nWhen a worker invokes `ddx bead create` from inside its execute-bead worktree, the resulting bead IDs are malformed. Reproduced today on this project:\n\n  /home/erik/.local/bin/ddx bead ready  → 11 entries with IDs like\n  `.execute-bead-wt-ddx-44236615-20260429T185004-ffa9adf1-e630aeae`\n\nThese were the read-coverage child beads spawned from the ddx-44236615 worker (commit ebeb2464). They have valid descriptions, AC, priorities, and labels — only the ID field is mangled. The pattern `.execute-bead-wt-\u003cparent\u003e-\u003ctimestamp\u003e-\u003cshort\u003e-\u003crandom\u003e` matches the worktree directory name format used by `ddx agent execute-bead`. Most likely failure path: the bead-id resolver consults `os.Getwd()` (or equivalent) to derive a project context prefix, sees the worktree dir, and uses it instead of the real project root + a fresh `ddx-\u003c8hex\u003e` short hash.\n\nConcrete impact:\n- 50+ char IDs are unwieldy in CLI commands (`ddx bead show \u003cid\u003e`, `ddx bead dep add ...`).\n- Cross-project readers and the bead store assume `ddx-\u003c8hex\u003e` prefix matching for tooling (filtering, GraphQL adapters, MCP responses) — these long IDs may sort or filter unexpectedly.\n- The IDs encode ephemeral attempt timestamps, which leaks worker-internal state into a permanent identifier.\n- 12 such beads exist in the tracker as of this filing (grep `.execute-bead-wt-` in `.ddx/beads.jsonl`).\n\n### Layer 2 (design question — separate decision)\n\nEven if the IDs were correctly formed `ddx-\u003c8hex\u003e`, it is not obvious that `ddx bead create` from inside an execute-bead worker is the right pattern at all. Two alternatives the design has not articulated a position on:\n\n**(a) Append to the parent bead.** When the worker discovers sub-tasks, it appends them as structured items in the parent's description, notes, or a new `kind:discovered-subtask` event. The parent bead becomes the durable record of \"what this run found\"; future runs (or the operator) can decompose if/when the work warrants its own tracker entries.\n\n**(b) Surface via the execute-bead result.** The agent emits a structured `discovered_subtasks: [...]` field on result.json; the loop / operator decides whether to file new beads. Keeps tracker mutations gated by an explicit decision point.\n\n**(c) Create new beads in-band (current behavior).** The worker calls `ddx bead create` directly. Pro: fastest decomposition. Con: sub-tasks land in the queue without operator review; can flood the queue if the agent over-decomposes; complicates \"what was THIS run's contribution\" auditing.\n\nToday's behavior is (c) but undocumented. The 11 read-coverage children landing as a single agent's decomposition is exactly the failure mode (c) makes easy: one analysis bead spawned 11 implementation beads in a single run, all P0, all without operator review. The work may be valid — but the pattern needs a position.\n\n## In-scope outcomes\n\nThis bead's job is to surface both layers; the implementation work decomposes into follow-ups.\n\n1. **Concrete fix for layer 1**: bead-id resolver should normalize worktree paths to the real project root before allocating an ID. Test: `ddx bead create` invoked with cwd=`\u003crepo\u003e/.execute-bead-wt-...` produces `ddx-\u003c8hex\u003e`, not `.execute-bead-wt-...-\u003crandom\u003e`.\n2. **Mass repair for the 12 existing malformed beads**: either rewrite their IDs to fresh `ddx-\u003c8hex\u003e` (with a redirect or alias map for any external references), or close them as deferred and re-file with proper IDs. Decision deferred until layer 2 is settled.\n3. **Layer 2 decision**: file a separate design bead (or amendment to FEAT-006 / FEAT-010) that picks (a), (b), or (c) and documents the rationale. This bead does NOT pre-decide; it captures the question.\n\n## In-scope files (layer 1 fix)\n\n- `cli/cmd/bead_create.go` (or wherever bead-create resolves project context).\n- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` or `cli/internal/bead/id.go` if ID generation lives there.\n- A unit test that drops cwd into a worktree-shaped dir and asserts the resulting bead id matches `^ddx-[0-9a-f]{8}$`.\n\n(Locate via: `grep -rn \"execute-bead-wt\\|FindProjectRoot\\|projectRoot\" cli/internal/bead/ cli/cmd/bead_*.go`.)\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- The 11 read-coverage child beads' actual implementation work — that's separate beads.\n- Default behavior change for whether workers can/should create beads — that's the layer-2 design bead.\n- GraphQL or MCP changes to handle long IDs — fix the source instead.\n\n## Acceptance\n\nLayer-1 only (this bead):\n\n1. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... ./internal/bead/...` passes after the fix.\n2. New unit test in `cli/internal/bead/` (or `cli/cmd/`) that simulates a bead-create call from `cwd=\u003cproject\u003e/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-XXXXX-\u003ctimestamp\u003e-\u003crandom\u003e` and asserts the new bead's `id` field matches `^ddx-[0-9a-f]{8}$`. Test fails before the fix, passes after.\n3. `grep -E '\"id\":\"\\.execute-bead-wt-' .ddx/beads.jsonl` returns either 0 (mass repair) or 12 with a recorded decision in this bead's notes that the existing 12 are deferred to a follow-up bead.\n4. A separate bead is filed (or referenced existing one updated) that captures the layer-2 design question (a/b/c). Reference its ID in this bead's events.\n\n## Why one bead, not an epic\n\nLayer 1 is a tight 1-3 file fix. Layer 2 is a design discussion that should be its own artifact, not a sibling task. Splitting them keeps each bounded.","events_attachment":"ddx-7eab13a6/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-30T00:59:34.510624284Z","id":"ddx-7eab13a6","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:bead","area:agent","kind:bug","kind:design"],"notes":"Layer-1 (ID resolver bug) fixed in this bead: detectPrefix() now takes a workingDir parameter and uses it for git commands instead of defaulting to cwd. This prevents linked worktrees from contaminating the prefix.\n\n22 existing malformed beads with .execute-bead-wt-* IDs are DEFERRED to a mass-repair follow-up. Decision pending on layer-2 design question (ddx-d3a4f89c) — once the in-band vs. append vs. result-field question is decided, the repair approach will be clearer. The malformed beads have valid descriptions, AC, priorities, and labels; only their IDs are wrong.\n\nFix in store.go correctly threads workingDir through detectPrefix and resolves the worktree-cwd contamination. However, the new test does not simulate the bug scenario: it never chdirs into a worktree-shaped path, so it would pass on main too — failing AC #2's 'fails-before-fix, passes-after' requirement. .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:432:{"acceptance":"- [ ] New function gitPushLanded(projectRoot, defaultBranch) called after successful CloseWithEvidence in execute_bead_loop.go\n- [ ] Push uses --ff-only; never --force or --force-with-lease\n- [ ] Push rejection triggers land_conflict + unclaim + refs/ddx/iterations/ preservation + stop-the-loop\n- [ ] Network failure triggers 'push_pending' attempt marker but does NOT fail the bead; subsequent workers on the same branch see the pending state and halt\n- [ ] Unit tests: successful push, rejected push, network failure\n- [ ] Integration test: two workers racing, one wins the push, the other is rejected and unclaims its bead\n- [ ] Integration test: repeated loops push each successful bead immediately (origin advances after every success)\n- [ ] Docs updated: success path includes push; land_conflict path is the canonical 'two machines raced' outcome","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:19:32.504244855Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-83ec1430","depends_on_id":"ddx-bd42f640","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:21:51Z"}],"description":"Second child of ddx-b7ee0d6f. When a worker successfully lands a bead (status = success), the new commit sits on the local branch forever. There is no git push. The source in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_commit.go has ZERO references to push/fetch/origin (grep verified). Result: successful work on one machine is invisible to every other machine, and the local branch grows indefinitely with unsynced commits.\n\n## Evidence\n\ncli/internal/agent/execute_bead_commit.go defines the commit step but never calls git push. The only mention of 'git' in that file is a comment about trailers.\n\ncli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go at the success branch (around line 343):\n\n    if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess {\n        if err := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.ResultRev); err != nil {\n            return result, err\n        }\n        result.Successes++\n        result.LastSuccessAt = now().UTC()\n    }\n\nThe tracker is updated (CloseWithEvidence writes to beads.jsonl) but the working tree / commits are never pushed to origin.\n\n## Required behavior\n\nAfter a successful landing (merge outcome = 'merged' AND bead status = success), the worker must:\n\n1. Run `git push --ff-only origin \u003cdefault-branch\u003e`\n2. On rejection:\n   - Classify as ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict (this status already exists in execute_bead_status.go)\n   - Preserve the commits under refs/ddx/iterations/ (the infrastructure already exists for the preserved-result case)\n   - Unclaim the bead so a later attempt can pick it up again AFTER divergence is resolved\n   - Emit a 'push_rejected' event with the local HEAD and the remote tip\n   - Stop the worker loop (divergence requires human attention)\n3. On network failure: similar to fetch — log a warning, mark the attempt with a 'push_pending' flag, continue\n4. Pair this push with the fetch-first bead (sibling ddx-bd42f640) so the loop is: fetch → verify ancestry → run bead → push — a closed consistency loop\n\n## Why --ff-only\n\nForce-with-lease is tempting but wrong here — if push is rejected, it means another machine pushed in the meantime, and we want to detect that and reconcile, not overwrite. --ff-only ensures we only ever advance origin, never rewrite it. A rejection is a signal, not a problem to work around.\n\n## Test cases\n\n- Fresh clone, worker lands a bead, push succeeds, origin advances to new HEAD\n- Another machine pushed between fetch and commit (race): second worker lands, push is rejected, bead is unclaimed and marked land_conflict, loop stops\n- Network unreachable: push is deferred, attempt records 'push_pending', worker continues but subsequent workers halt on the same branch\n- Default branch is configurable","id":"ddx-83ec1430","issue_type":"bug","labels":["bug","execute-loop","git","orchestration"],"parent":"ddx-b7ee0d6f","priority":0,"session_id":"eb-43009cc","status":"closed","title":"execute-loop: push to origin on successful bead landing","updated_at":"2026-04-14T15:26:48.60994229Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:441:{"acceptance":"A single per-project land coordinator goroutine owns all writes to target refs. Unit tests in cli/internal/agent/ (or cli/internal/server/) prove: (1) happy path — worker submits base=A result=B, coordinator fetches current tip X, rebases B onto X, fast-forwards, asserts the new tip is the rebased commit and no merge commits exist in the history between A and the new tip (use git log --merges --count to assert 0); (2) rebase conflict path — worker submits a commit that cannot rebase cleanly, coordinator preserves the submission under refs/ddx/iterations/\u003cbead-id\u003e/\u003cattempt-id\u003e and returns a \"preserved\" status, target branch is unchanged; (3) serialization path — 5 concurrent submissions against the same project against base=A produce 5 sequential rebased commits on the target branch with linear history (git log --merges returns 0), and the coordinator processes them in order; (4) server runWorker integration — a server-managed worker whose task produces a commit B actually advances the target branch to a rebased B, via the coordinator path, visible in git log main -1 after the worker exits; (5) --local mode integration — the --local execute-loop path runs a process-local coordinator that provides the same semantics. Secondary acceptance: RealOrchestratorGitOps.Merge is deleted from execute_bead_orchestrator.go OR clearly marked as the legacy path to remove; no production code path calls git merge --no-edit on the target branch; FEAT-006, API-001, FEAT-012 still describe the same contract (rebase + ff-only, linear history). Operator-visible smoke: running ddx agent execute-loop --harness claude --model sonnet against a seeded queue produces a linear history on main with no merge commits between the run's first and last commit.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-14T14:35:57.684173463Z","description":"Today's execute-loop orchestration is a pile of patches on a flawed mental model. The user's target model is \"human picks PR off the queue → rebases onto current tip → merges → next worker starts from the new tip.\" The code implements \"worker branches off whatever main was when it started → runs task → git merge --no-edit that result back → 2-parent merge commit fan.\" The two models are incompatible; no patch to the second model gets you to the first.\n\nEvidence the current model is wrong:\n\n1. FEAT-006 §\"Landing\" (line 414), API-001 §\"Landing \u0026 Preservation\" (lines 217-222), and FEAT-012 §\"Execute-Bead Git Operations\" (line 413) all explicitly require rebase + fast-forward with linear history. The code in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:61 Merge() runs `git merge --no-edit` with a defensive `chore: checkpoint before merge` commit — zero rebase, zero ff-only, zero fetch, zero push. Three spec files aligned on one model; code implements a different one.\n\n2. cli/internal/server/workers.go:runWorker (around line 237) calls agent.ExecuteBead via an inline ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc closure at workers.go:248 and never calls LandBeadResult. LandBeadResult is only wired into two call sites: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1372 (the `--local` execute-loop path) and cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:134 (the interactive single-bead CLI). The default `ddx agent execute-loop` path (which submits to the server) produces worker commits that are NEVER landed on main. Commits sit in worktree refs and the loop reports \"success\" to the tracker anyway.\n\n3. The \"worker simplification\" commit 49c3d2b that closed ddx-679d2fc7 moved Merge/UpdateRef out of execute_bead.go's GitOps interface into a new OrchestratorGitOps in execute_bead_orchestrator.go. That is a code-level function split, not a process-level split. The \"parent orchestrator\" runs synchronously in the worker process from whichever call site reaches it first. Two workers racing on the same projectRoot will both call LandBeadResult with stale baseRev assumptions; the current Merge implementation papers over conflicts with ort-strategy merge commits. The user was explicit in their feedback: \"we may not be separating concerns appropriately — we have pushed too much functionality into execute-loop and it's causing us issues; it needs to be very simple: 'I've been handed a worktree and a task, I will execute that task and make commits, produce progress output, notify done.'\" The current code does not reflect that intent.\n\n4. Six open child beads of ddx-b7ee0d6f (bd42f640 fetch-before-claim, 6aa50e57 rebase-instead-of-merge, 83ec1430 push-on-land, 2791dc4f tracker concurrency, 42dd982c divergence monitoring, b0374901 multi-machine docs) each correctly identify one piece of the gap but do not compose into a coherent end-to-end workflow. The fix is not \"implement all six\"; the fix is to adopt the human-PR model as the single source of truth and then let those beads fall out as sub-tasks of a coherent design.\n\n## Target design\n\nThe human-PR mental model, encoded as code:\n\n1. **Worker.** Accepts a pre-made worktree and a prompt. Runs the agent harness. Emits structured progress events. Produces zero or more commits in the worktree. Exits with a simple terminal status (task_succeeded / task_failed / task_no_changes) and reports its worktree branch + commit SHAs to the server. The worker NEVER fetches, rebases, merges, pushes, or writes a ref. The worker NEVER interacts with target refs on the shared project repo. The worker does not care what main is doing.\n\n2. **Land coordinator.** A single goroutine per project (hosted on the ddx server) that owns all writes to the project's target refs. Workers submit their completed worktree branch SHAs to the coordinator via a server endpoint. The coordinator, for each submission, in order: (a) `git fetch origin \u003ctarget-branch\u003e` to refresh the target tip; (b) `git rebase --onto \u003ctarget-tip\u003e \u003cbase-rev\u003e \u003cresult-rev\u003e` to replay the worker's commits onto the latest tip; (c) if the rebase is clean, `git push --ff-only origin \u003ctarget-branch\u003e` (or equivalent local update-ref if no remote); (d) on rebase conflict or push rejection, move the submission to `refs/ddx/iterations/\u003cbead-id\u003e/\u003cattempt-id\u003e` and mark the bead as needing human review. The coordinator handles one submission at a time. No locks needed internally because there's only one goroutine.\n\n3. **Worker-to-coordinator handoff.** Workers submit to a new `POST /api/agent/workers/:id/land-submission` endpoint with {worktree_branch, base_rev, result_rev, bead_id}. The endpoint enqueues the submission onto the land coordinator's channel and returns 202 Accepted. Workers block (via long-polling GET, SSE, or channel-await) for the land result — success (pushed to target), preserved (moved to iteration ref), or failed (rejected, worker should retry from new tip).\n\n4. **Worker isolation.** Workers run in /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/ worktrees per the alpha14 worktree-relocation fix. They fetch their starting base from the coordinator (\"what is the current tip?\"), branch off it, work, and submit. They never touch the shared project .git beyond their own worktree's read-fetch.\n\n5. **Tracker (beads.jsonl) coordination.** Separate concern from the git target refs. ddx-2791dc4f stays as its own bead because the tracker is a second shared resource with its own write-serialization requirement. The land coordinator and the tracker lock must both be respected, but they are orthogonal locks.\n\n6. **Multi-machine.** When the server grows to multi-machine topology, each machine runs its own land coordinator against its local clone of the project. Coordinators push to a common origin. The origin becomes the single source of truth for the target branch. Rebase-then-push gives the per-machine coordinators a clean serialization point via origin's ref update (push is atomic at the remote).\n\n## What this replaces\n\n- ddx-bd42f640 (fetch+ancestry check before claim) → subsumed. The land coordinator owns the fetch; workers don't need to check ancestry because they're handed a branch from the coordinator.\n- ddx-6aa50e57 (rebase instead of merge) → subsumed. The coordinator's rebase step is the canonical implementation.\n- ddx-83ec1430 (push-to-origin on landing) → subsumed. Push is part of the coordinator's sequence.\n- ddx-42dd982c (divergence monitoring) → reframed. Becomes monitoring of the coordinator's rejection rate and conflict rate.\n- ddx-b0374901 (multi-machine topology docs) → reframed. Document the per-machine coordinator + push-to-origin model.\n- The missing \"server runWorker never lands\" bug (new P0 child, filed alongside) → subsumed. The new model HAS a land step by design.\n\nWhat this does NOT replace:\n\n- ddx-2791dc4f (beads.jsonl concurrent-write safety) — separate concern, separate lock.\n- ddx-679d2fc7 (simplify worker to task-only) — this bead is nominally closed by commit 49c3d2b, but that commit did a cosmetic function split, not the architectural process split. Re-open or file a follow-up that cleans up the nomenclature (\"OrchestratorGitOps\" → \"CoordinatorGitOps\" scoped to the single coordinator goroutine) and deletes Merge() in favor of Land(). OR leave it closed and track that cleanup under this meta-bead.\n\n## Why this is better\n\nThe core insight: **target-ref writes must be serialized through a single writer that always sees the current tip.** That is how every real PR queue works (GitHub merge queue, gerrit, Zuul). The current design has every worker trying to be its own merge-master; the fix is to have exactly one merge-master per project and let workers be contributors.\n\n## Rollout\n\nThis is a redesign, not a patch. Rollout steps:\n\n1. Land a new Land(projectRoot, baseRev, resultRev, targetBranch) coordinator function that implements fetch → rebase → ff-push → preserve-on-fail. Unit tests that cover the happy path, the rebase conflict path, and the concurrent-submission serialization path.\n2. Add POST /api/agent/workers/:id/land-submission + the land coordinator goroutine + per-project queue channel to cli/internal/server/workers.go.\n3. Rewrite workers.go:runWorker to have the worker's executor closure submit to the local coordinator (via channel; no HTTP round-trip needed since it's in the same process) instead of calling LandBeadResult inline.\n4. Delete or mothball the current LandBeadResult and its \"orchestrator\" naming. The new coordinator replaces it.\n5. .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:456:{"acceptance":"Invoking `ddx bead` commands anywhere inside a repo subtree that contains a local `.ddx/` resolves to that repo's tracker; installed and source-built binaries behave the same; and regression tests cover repo root and nested subdirectory invocation without silent success against the wrong tracker","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"a2db669db91cca67f9316b854c5768963a8ee304","created_at":"2026-04-10T22:16:29.83752921Z","description":"\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\nReview area: tracker context resolution for ddx bead commands. Evidence covers the repo-local tracker contract in FEAT-004, the CLI project-root resolution rules, and the observed failure where an installed ddx binary invoked from /Users/erik/Projects/ddx returned bead IDs from `ddx bead create` but did not persist them into this repo's .ddx/beads.jsonl.\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\nMake bead commands resolve their tracker context deterministically from the nearest parent repository containing a .ddx directory, rather than drifting to another context or reporting success without updating the live tracker.\n\n## Scope\n- Reproduce and fix the context-resolution bug for `ddx bead create` and the rest of the bead command family\n- Nail down the repository-selection rule: nearest parent repo with a local `.ddx/` wins\n- Ensure installed and source-built binaries resolve the same tracker context when invoked from the same repo subtree\n- Add regression coverage for repo-root, subdirectory, and nested-worktree invocation paths\n\n## Boundaries\n- Do not change the rule that bead state is maintained via `ddx bead` commands rather than direct JSONL edits\n- Do not introduce hidden cross-project tracker selection heuristics","events_attachment":"ddx-8d32168a/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-8d32168a","issue_type":"bug","labels":["helix","phase:build","kind:implementation","area:cli","area:bead"],"notes":"Fixed in a2db669: nearest-parent .ddx workspace resolution now applies consistently to bead commands, with regression coverage.","priority":0,"spec-id":"FEAT-004","status":"closed","title":"Fix tracker context resolution for bead commands","updated_at":"2026-04-10T23:19:07.605046791Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:467:{"acceptance":"ddx agent workers prints a table with STATE, BEAD ID, TITLE, HARNESS, MODEL, ELAPSED, ATTEMPTS columns. Server-submitted workers show state from WorkerManager. Local --local workers are detected via manifest scan of .ddx/.execute-bead-wt-* directories. --json flag emits raw JSON. --watch re-renders every 2s. A worker executing bead axon-abc123 shows that bead ID and its title (truncated). An idle system (no active workers) prints 'no active workers' rather than an empty table. No dependency on worktrunk or any external binary beyond git.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-14T07:31:03Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1412283","closing_commit_sha":"3f16c49fc95957923d3f45fc2a93ec647ba859b4","created_at":"2026-04-13T20:34:44.901809653Z","description":"There is no CLI command to see which workers are running and which bead each is executing. The WorkerManager in cli/internal/server/workers.go already tracks this (WorkerRecord.CurrentBead at line 51, WorkerRecord.State, WorkerRecord.Harness, WorkerRecord.Model, WorkerRecord.Attempts/Successes/Failures) but it is only accessible through the server API. Local --local workers are not tracked by the server at all.\n\nThis bead adds ddx agent workers as a top-level CLI subcommand. It must not depend on worktrunk (wt) or any external tool.\n\n## Required changes\n\n### 1. New command: ddx agent workers (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go)\nRegister alongside the existing ddx agent log command. Flags:\n  --json         emit raw JSON array\n  --watch        re-render every 2s (like watch -n2) until Ctrl-C\n  --project      filter to a specific project root (default: CWD project)\n\n### 2. Data sources (two paths)\nA. Server-submitted workers: GET http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/agent/workers — deserialize []WorkerRecord from the server. Already implemented in cli/internal/server/workers.go List() / HTTP handler.\nB. Local --local workers: the server does not track these. Detect them by:\n   1. Reading .ddx/workers/*/status.json files in the project root (server writes these even for local workers when they self-register — confirm in workers.go; if not, fall back to scanning active execute-bead worktrees)\n   2. Scanning .ddx/.execute-bead-wt-* directories (from ExecuteBeadWtPrefix constant in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go line 403) and reading the manifest.json inside each to extract bead_id, attempt_id, harness, model, started_at\n\n### 3. Local worker detection via manifest scan\nFor each directory matching .ddx/.execute-bead-wt-*:\n  - Read .ddx/executions/{attemptID}/manifest.json (path encoded in worktree name suffix)\n  - Check if worktree is still active (git worktree list --porcelain)\n  - Read execute-loop-heartbeat-at from bead.Extra (requires ddx-7651506a) if available\n  - Derive synthetic WorkerRecord from manifest fields\n\n### 4. Table output (default)\nColumns: STATE | BEAD | TITLE (truncated 40 chars) | HARNESS | MODEL | ELAPSED | ATTEMPTS\n- STATE: running (green), exited (dim), failed (red)\n- ELAPSED: human-readable (e.g., 4m32s)\n- If current bead title unavailable (local worker pre-manifest), show bead ID\n\n### 5. Merge and deduplicate\nWhen both server and manifest sources are available, deduplicate by worker_id or attempt_id. Server record takes precedence over manifest-derived record for the same worker.\n\n## Key files\n- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go — add newAgentWorkersCommand(), register in newAgentCommand()\n- cli/internal/server/workers.go — WorkerRecord struct (lines 31-54), List API\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go — ExecuteBeadWtPrefix constant (line 403), manifest shape (lines 726-771)\n- cli/internal/agent/types.go — SessionEntry Correlation map (bead_id field)","events_attachment":"ddx-9060c8b2/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-14T07:45:03.73510309Z","id":"ddx-9060c8b2","issue_type":"task","labels":["ux","execute-loop","observability","cli"],"owner":"ddx","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-reconcile-3f16c49","status":"closed","title":"Add ddx agent workers command — native live worker status with current bead","updated_at":"2026-04-14T14:12:47.499704451Z"}
